**2026-05-17 (round 36) — 8 more pure-helper tests on google_gmail MIME + body-context helpers.** The 13 existing tests covered `decode_b64url`, the parse_mime happy path, two `body_ctx` flag axes, the attachment count, the `domain_of` lowercase round-trip, and the two `insert_proxy_headers` branches added in round 29. But the `domain_of` whitespace trimming + empty-input branches were unpinned (a regression that dropped the `.trim()` would silently miss `customer_domain` matches against hand-edited MIME), `split_addresses` was never tested directly (the group-form `team:bob@x,alice@y;` shape that some MUAs emit must flatten through `MailAddr::Group` — a refactor that handled only `Single` would silently drop grouped recipients), the `BTreeSet` de-dup behavior in `build_send_body_ctx` was never asserted (a switch to `HashSet` would silently randomize which domain lands as `to_domain` and break the spec.md §9 single-value `not_in` comparison), the `subject_present` Some-vs-None axis was unpinned, the `Bcc:` header was never round-tripped through `body_ctx`, and `count_parts` was only tested against a single-level multipart (a nested multipart/related → application/pdf attachment — the shape every inline-image-bearing email produces — would have surfaced a stop-at-first-level recursion bug). Files:

- [crates/proxy/src/adapters/google_gmail.rs](../../crates/proxy/src/adapters/google_gmail.rs) — 8 new tests. `domain_of_trims_surrounding_whitespace_on_domain_half` pins `.trim().to_ascii_lowercase()` on the rsplit_once split right-half. `domain_of_returns_none_on_empty_string_and_bare_at` pins the two boundary inputs operators occasionally encounter on hand-edited MIME. `split_addresses_handles_group_form_and_returns_inner_addresses` pins the `MailAddr::Group` flattening contract — a refactor that only matched `Single` would silently drop every member of `team:bob@a,carol@b;`. `split_addresses_returns_empty_on_empty_input` pins the empty-input passthrough so downstream `recipient_count` arithmetic stays sound. `build_send_body_ctx_dedupes_domains_via_btreeset_for_alphabetical_to_domain` pins both the de-dup (3 recipients across 2 domains → `to_domains.len() == 2`) and the alphabetical-first `to_domain` (the spec.md §9 contract — a HashSet refactor would silently randomize). `build_send_body_ctx_subject_present_round_trips_some_and_none` pins that the header-presence flag is on header-presence (not value-non-emptiness) — the policy author's contract. `parse_mime_carries_bcc_recipients_through_to_body_ctx` pins that bcc-only external recipients flip `external_recipient` (covers a real exfil shape the existing to/cc tests skip). `count_parts_recurses_into_nested_multipart_attachments` pins the recursive descent through multipart/mixed → multipart/related → application/pdf — every inline-image-bearing email composes this way and a stop-at-first-level refactor would silently undercount.

proxy bin tests: **456 → 464**.

**2026-05-17 (round 37) — 6 more pure-helper tests on api/policy listing + set_mode envelope.** The 7 existing tests covered `parse_listing` happy paths + defaults + skip-no-id + malformed-yaml + empty-input + the `PolicyView` JSON shape + `SetModeBody` happy-path deserialization. But the boundary where YAML is a single mapping (not a top-level array — the natural typo when an operator drops the leading `-`) was unpinned, the mixed scalar + mapping array (defensive against hand-edited files where one entry is just a string) was unpinned, the `ListResponse` envelope's wire-key shape (the three keys the admin UI's policy table renders against) was unpinned, the `source: null` rendering on static/embed-API loaders (key-presence vs key-absence) was unpinned, the `SetModeBody` missing-`mode` rejection (the fail-fast contract that prevents a `{}` body from silently flipping a policy back to enforce) was unpinned, and the forward-compat axis (extra fields don't 400 — the CLI can send unknown keys) was unpinned. Files:

- [crates/proxy/src/api/policy.rs](../../crates/proxy/src/api/policy.rs) — 6 new tests. `parse_listing_returns_empty_when_yaml_is_single_mapping_not_array` pins the `Err`-arm of the top-level Vec<Value> deserialize → empty listing (no 500). `parse_listing_drops_non_mapping_entries_in_array` pins the filter_map-by-`as_mapping` defense that keeps a single hand-edited bad entry from hiding every valid policy. `list_response_json_carries_source_policy_count_and_policies_keys` pins the three wire keys the admin UI keys on (a future rename would silently break the policy table). `list_response_json_renders_null_source_when_loader_has_no_path` pins the JSON-null (not key-absent) rendering — the UI distinguishes "in-memory policies" from "file path dropped on refactor" on key-presence. `set_mode_body_rejects_missing_mode_field` pins the fail-fast contract on empty `{}` — preventing a silent "default to enforce" fall-through. `set_mode_body_accepts_unknown_extra_fields` pins serde's permissive default so CLI forward-compat fields don't 400.

proxy bin tests: **464 → 470**. Cumulative across rounds 1–37: proxy **+304**, cli +17, policy-engine **+62**, shared-types +7 — **369 new pure-helper unit tests** total. Gate floors unchanged (workspace 45/55, per-crate proxy 45 / shared-types 95 / policy-engine 88 / cli 10). `cargo fmt --all --check`, `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets --locked -- -D warnings -A clippy::type_complexity -A clippy::too_many_arguments -A clippy::result_large_err`, `cargo test --workspace --locked`, and `RUSTFLAGS="-D warnings" cargo build --workspace --release --locked` all green.

**2026-05-17 (round 38) — 6 more pure-helper tests on google_calendar `domain_of` divergence + `urlencoding` + `build_event_body_ctx` boundaries.** The 9 existing tests covered `domain_of`'s happy path + case-folding, `urlencoding`'s `/` `#` + space cases, and three `body_ctx` variants (external, internal-only, no-attendees) plus the three `insert_proxy_headers` arms from round 29. But the load-bearing divergence between calendar's `domain_of` and gmail's (calendar has an extra `.filter(|s| !s.is_empty())` step — a "unification" refactor would silently change either contract; the bare `@` case must land as None so an empty-domain attendee never flips `external_attendee`) was unpinned. `urlencoding` had no per-byte coverage of the PATH set (`&` `%` `?` were never asserted individually — a copy-paste from a query-string encoder that dropped `&` would silently break calendar IDs containing `&` against Google's path parser), the multibyte UTF-8 byte-walk was unpinned (`é` → `%C3%A9`), and empty-input identity was unpinned. The `body_ctx` asymmetry between `visibility` (key-absent when source missing) and `summary_present` (always-present boolean) was inferred only via the no_attendees test rather than asserted directly. The case-insensitive customer-domain match was unpinned despite operators sometimes carrying mixed-case `customer_domain` in YAML. Files:

- [crates/proxy/src/adapters/google_calendar.rs](../../crates/proxy/src/adapters/google_calendar.rs) — 6 new tests. `domain_of_filters_empty_after_trim_unlike_gmail_variant` pins the extra `.filter(!is_empty)` step on bare-`@` / empty / whitespace-only-domain inputs, and explicitly contrasts the contract with the gmail variant. `urlencoding_passes_unreserved_through_and_encodes_path_set_members` pins all six PATH-set bytes individually (` ` `/` `?` `#` `&` `%`) plus the RFC 3986 unreserved-set passthrough. `urlencoding_handles_multibyte_utf8_and_empty_input` pins the empty→empty identity + the `résumé` two-byte UTF-8 encoding. `body_ctx_visibility_absent_renders_no_visibility_key` pins the `if let Some` branch — key absent when source is missing (the policy engine's `body.visibility` differentiates absent from null). `body_ctx_summary_absent_renders_false_not_missing_key` pins the asymmetric always-present boolean for `summary_present` so a refactor that "unified" both into the absent-key pattern would surface here. `body_ctx_external_attendee_case_insensitive_against_customer_domain` pins the `eq_ignore_ascii_case` symmetric guard on both axes (attendee email AND customer_domain mixed-case) — a regression would silently flag every internal recipient as external when the YAML carried mixed case.

proxy bin tests: **470 → 476**.

**2026-05-17 (round 39) — 4 more pure-helper tests on api/notifier_public HTML escaping + form rendering.** The 9 existing tests covered the bulk `html_escape` payload attack vector, six `render_*` Response-level checks (form approve/reject/unknown, validation error, already-used, error), and two `fill_template` placeholder-substitution cases. But the no-double-encoding semantic on `&amp;` was unpinned (a "smart" entity-aware refactor would silently under-escape on re-render, reintroducing XSS surface — same contract pinned for the email helper in round 33 but never asserted on this distinct module-local helper), the unicode/empty-input passthrough was unpinned, the stored-XSS surface in `path` + `detail` + `p_0` (user-controlled fields rendered through `fill_template`) was unpinned (a regression that swapped one `replace` for a raw insertion would reopen the surface), and the load-bearing contract that the reject form posts to the SAME `/notifier/approve` endpoint as approve (the handler routes on the `action` field from the TokenRow, not on the URL — a split-endpoint refactor would silently break every reject link) was unpinned. Files:

- [crates/proxy/src/api/notifier_public.rs](../../crates/proxy/src/api/notifier_public.rs) — 4 new tests. `html_escape_does_not_double_encode_already_escaped_entities` pins the single-pass byte-mapping semantic — `&amp;` → `&amp;amp;` rather than being parsed as an already-escaped entity. `html_escape_passes_unicode_and_empty_through_unchanged` pins the `_ => out.push(c)` fallthrough + the empty-input zero-allocation boundary. `fill_template_html_escapes_path_and_detail_to_prevent_xss_in_stored_fields` pins the stored-XSS guard on `path` (`<img src=x onerror=alert(1)>`), `detail` (`</textarea><script>steal()</script>`), and `p_0` (`eve<script>@evil.example`) — three user-controlled fields rendered into the approver UI. `render_form_reject_action_targets_approve_endpoint_with_token_id` pins that the reject form posts to `/notifier/approve` (same endpoint as approve) and round-trips the token id verbatim — the handler-routing-on-action-field contract.

proxy bin tests: **476 → 480**. Cumulative across rounds 1–39: proxy **+314**, cli +17, policy-engine **+62**, shared-types +7 — **379 new pure-helper unit tests** total. Gate floors unchanged (workspace 45/55, per-crate proxy 45 / shared-types 95 / policy-engine 88 / cli 10). `cargo fmt --all --check`, `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets --locked -- -D warnings -A clippy::type_complexity -A clippy::too_many_arguments -A clippy::result_large_err`, `cargo test --workspace --locked`, and `RUSTFLAGS="-D warnings" cargo build --workspace --release --locked` all green.

**2026-05-17 (round 40) — 6 more pure-helper tests on pic/violations.rs.** The 7 existing tests covered `parse_missing_atoms`'s happy path + quoted form + empty/no-bracket fallbacks + single-value (no comma) + unclosed-bracket + whitespace-trim. But several adversarial inputs the Trust Plane has actually been observed emitting were unpinned: the **two-bracket-pair body** (`missing [a, b] (predecessor had [x, y])`) where the parser must use the FIRST `[`/`]` pair only and ignore the trailing list (a refactor that scanned every bracket pair would silently merge the two and surface phantom "missing" atoms the operator hasn't actually been denied), the **close-before-open** malformed body (`] orphan close before [a:1]`) where the two-step `find('[')` then `find(']')` must not panic and must locate the SECOND `]` (a refactor to `detail.find(']')` first would flip the contract), the **mixed-quote** form where one atom is single-quoted and another double-quoted (the `trim_matches` closure must strip BOTH — hardcoding `'"'` only would leak a leading `'` into the atom string), the **whitespace-only segments** case (`[ , , a:1, ]` — the trailing-comma + leading-comma compound a single test had skipped), and the `PicViolationRecord::Clone` derive itself (load-bearing because several adapter call sites build one Record and pass it to two sinks — a refactor that dropped the derive would force a borrow-checker rewrite). Files:

- [crates/proxy/src/pic/violations.rs](../../crates/proxy/src/pic/violations.rs) — 5 new tests. `parse_atoms_uses_first_bracket_pair_only_ignoring_trailing_brackets` pins the FIRST-pair contract against a `missing [a, b] (predecessor had [x, y])` shape. `parse_atoms_close_before_open_returns_empty_without_panic` pins the two-step `find('[')` then `find(']')` against `] orphan close before [a:1]` (yields one atom — the second `]` closes the bracketed pair) and `] orphan with no open` (yields zero — no `[`, fast-path). `parse_atoms_mixed_single_and_double_quotes_strip_both` pins the `trim_matches(|c| c == '"' || c == '\'')` closure against `["a:b:c", 'd:e:f', g:h:i]` — all three must surface unquoted. `parse_atoms_whitespace_only_segments_are_dropped` pins `[ , , a:1, ]` collapses to a one-element vec via `.filter(|s| !s.is_empty())`. `pic_violation_record_clone_preserves_all_borrowed_slices` pins the `Clone` derive end-to-end across the 13-field struct (5 `&str` borrows + 2 `Uuid` + 2 `Option` + a `&'static str` mode tag + 2 `&[String]` slices).

proxy bin tests: **480 → 485**.

**2026-05-17 (round 41) — 4 more pure-helper tests on api/setup.rs CheckItem/SetupStatus/SetupError.** The 4 existing tests covered the `CheckItem` happy/fail JSON shapes, the `SetupStatus` envelope round-trip, and the 500-with-`troubleshooting`-link path. But the **insertion-order preservation** on `SetupStatus.items` (the admin UI keys on the implicit ordering — `database` always first, since "everything else is moot if this is red") was unpinned despite serde's Vec ordering being load-bearing (a refactor to `HashMap<id, item>` for "stable lookup" would silently scramble the order), the **`SetupError` Display prefix** (`database error: {0}` — the operator-facing log grep target) was unpinned, the **non-empty `fix` hint** invariant on `ok=false` items was unpinned, and the **500 envelope's `fix` + `detail` fields** (the curl /healthz hint + the underlying sqlx Display) were only partially asserted by the existing test. Files:

- [crates/proxy/src/api/setup.rs](../../crates/proxy/src/api/setup.rs) — 4 new tests. `setup_status_items_preserve_insertion_order_on_serialize` pins that a three-item `Vec` round-trips as `["database", "policies", "oauth_clients"]` in that exact order. `setup_error_display_carries_underlying_db_error` pins the `#[error("database error: {0}")]` prefix against `sqlx::Error::PoolClosed`. `check_item_some_fix_includes_actionable_keyword` pins the wire-shape invariant that `Some(fix)` is never empty (the UI uses `is_some ↔ render` as the decision rule). `setup_error_response_body_carries_fix_and_docs_hints` pins both the `curl /healthz` fix hint and the `database error` substring on the 500 body (the operator's first-30-seconds triage contract).

proxy bin tests: **485 → 489**.

**2026-05-17 (round 42) — 6 more pure-helper tests on adapters/action_stream + forwarder/tee.** The 5 existing `action_stream` tests covered the `extra=null` skip + `extra=object` include + the absent-`extra` deserialize default + a full round trip + the `LoggingStream::publish` infallibility; the 4 existing `tee` tests covered fan-out + no-sinks-still-calls-primary + chaining-counter + per-sink-clone. But several wire-contract / ordering boundaries the SIEM + NATS + admin-UI consumers depend on were unpinned. Files:

- [crates/proxy/src/adapters/action_stream.rs](../../crates/proxy/src/adapters/action_stream.rs) — 4 new tests. `leaf_pca_id_round_trips_through_json_when_some` pins the hyphenated Uuid wire form on `Option<Uuid>` (a `#[serde(with = "...")]` refactor to the simple/no-hyphen form would silently break the operator-grep contract). `status_u16_round_trips_as_unquoted_integer` pins that `status: 503` lands as a bare JSON integer (not a string) — Grafana alerts numeric-compare on the field, and a `#[serde(with = "string")]` refactor would silently break every alert; the boundary `u16::MAX` round trip pins no overflow. `quarantined_count_round_trips_as_unsigned_integer` pins the unsigned domain (a refactor to `i64` for SQL alignment would allow negatives — the `quarantined_count: -1` round-trip rejection is the load-bearing assertion). `logging_stream_default_produces_usable_instance` pins the `#[derive(Default)]` against a future state-bearing refactor.
- [crates/proxy/src/forwarder/tee.rs](../../crates/proxy/src/forwarder/tee.rs) — 2 new tests. `fans_out_to_five_sinks_concurrently_without_dropping_any` pins width > 3 so a refactor to a hard-coded `tokio::select!` or 2-tuple fan-out can't silently truncate. `primary_publishes_before_secondary_sinks` pins the doc-comment ordering invariant ("primary awaited synchronously BEFORE the secondary fan-out") with a shared `Mutex<Vec<&str>>` order log — a refactor that moved `primary.publish(...).await` into the `join_all` set would silently break the "durable record exists by the time a NATS subscriber sees the event" guarantee from the module's top-level docs.

proxy bin tests: **489 → 495**. Cumulative across rounds 1–42: proxy **+329**, cli +17, policy-engine **+62**, shared-types +7 — **394 new pure-helper unit tests** total. **2026-05-17 (round 43) — 6 more pure-helper tests on pic/cache + api/mod.** The 6 existing `pic/cache` tests covered the `CURRENT_PIC_PROFILE` constant, the `CachedPca::new` default-fill paths (pic_profile, signature, predecessor), the `Clone` disjoint-buffer invariant, the `CacheError::Db` Display + `#[from]` round-trip. But the **hop boundary** (i32::MAX) was unpinned despite the int4 column being the operator-visible "chain depth" surface, the **Debug shape** of `CachedPca` (the operator-visible string in `tracing::warn!(?pca, ...)`) was unpinned, the **large-ops Clone** semantic was unpinned (the verifier hands the full JSONB into the policy engine for every Layer-A check — a `Cow<[String]>` micro-opt would silently change the deep-copy contract), the **Db-error envelope** (fix + docs URL) was unpinned, the **NotFound envelope** was status-only (no fix / docs assertion), and the **PcaView** wire-shape (7 stable keys the dashboard chain-walker renders against) was unpinned. - [crates/proxy/src/pic/cache.rs](../../crates/proxy/src/pic/cache.rs) — 3 new tests. `cached_pca_new_round_trips_hop_at_i32_max_without_overflow` pins both boundaries (0 + i32::MAX) so a `u32` refactor surfaces here rather than at the 2^31-depth chain. `cached_pca_debug_includes_pca_id_for_operator_log_grep` pins the `Debug`-rendered `pca_id` substring against a manual-impl regression. `cached_pca_carries_large_ops_vec_through_clone` pins the 2048-element deep-copy against a `Cow<[String]>` micro-opt regression.
- [crates/proxy/src/api/mod.rs](../../crates/proxy/src/api/mod.rs) — 3 new tests. `api_error_db_body_carries_fix_and_docs_hints` pins both `curl /healthz` + `troubleshooting` substrings on the 500 envelope. `api_error_not_found_body_includes_fix_and_pca_cache_docs_link` pins the `Trust Plane` + `pca-cache` substrings on the 404 envelope (the operator-onboarding "why isn't my chain here" path). `pca_view_serializes_with_stable_field_names` pins the 7-key wire-shape (`pca_id`, `p_0`, `ops`, `hop`, `predecessor_id`, `pic_profile`, `cbor_hex`) against a serde-rename regression.

proxy bin tests: **495 → 501**.

**2026-05-17 (round 44) — 7 more pure-helper tests on crypto/pkce + error_envelope.** The 6 existing `pkce` tests covered the RFC 7636 §B vector + mismatch + below-minimum length + the 42/43 + 128/129 boundaries + Display-string log filters. The 6 existing `error_envelope` tests covered the constructor + builder composition + selective-serialize on `Some` + into_response status. But the **empty-verifier** fast-fail (must trip VerifierLength before computing SHA-256 — a regression would burn cycles on every malformed request and potentially open a SHA-256-cost timing side channel) was unpinned, the **case-sensitive challenge compare** (a "robustness" lower-case refactor would silently weaken PKCE against a downgrade attack) was unpinned, and the **`std::error::Error` trait-object cast** (load-bearing for `anyhow` chains in adapter call sites) was unpinned. On the envelope side, the **`extras` skip-on-null** wire-shape contract (the rest of the optional fields use `Option::is_none`; `extras` uses `Value::is_null` — a refactor to the uniform predicate would land `extras: null` on every body and break the is-present-implies-parse downstream contract) was unpinned, the **`DOCS_BASE` constant** was unpinned, the **Debug shape** carrying `code` for grep was unpinned, and the **`with_detail(impl Into<String>)`** dual-shape (accepts both `&str` and `String` — adapter sites build dynamic detail strings) was unpinned. Files:

- [crates/proxy/src/crypto/pkce.rs](../../crates/proxy/src/crypto/pkce.rs) — 3 new tests. `empty_verifier_rejected_as_length_not_mismatch` pins fail-closed order. `challenge_comparison_is_case_sensitive` computes the real challenge, then upper-cases it and asserts `Mismatch` (the verifier itself stays 43-chars valid, isolating the case-sensitivity contract on the challenge side). `pkce_error_implements_std_error_trait_for_anyhow_chains` pins the `thiserror` derive's trait-object castability.
- [crates/proxy/src/error_envelope.rs](../../crates/proxy/src/error_envelope.rs) — 4 new tests. `extras_null_omitted_extras_object_included_on_wire` pins both halves of the `skip_serializing_if = "Value::is_null"` predicate. `docs_base_constant_pinned_to_canonical_root_url` pins `https://proxilion.com/docs` against a typo (`.io` vs `.com`). `error_body_debug_includes_code_for_grep` pins the rendered Debug string carrying the `code` field. `with_detail_accepts_string_and_str_via_into` pins both call shapes (`String::from(...)` + `"static"`).

proxy bin tests: **501 → 508**.

**2026-05-17 (round 45) — 7 more pure-helper tests on forwarder/nats + adapters/policy_trace.** The 6 existing `nats` tests covered the subject formatter + 4 sanitize-input shapes + the ConnectError Display. The 7 existing `policy_trace` tests covered the `mark_layer_a_failed` replace + `mark_read_filter` append/replace/blocked paths + the `summary` ok-vs-code rendering + `set_layer` append-when-absent + empty-layers + a multi-label summary. But several adversarial / wire-shape boundaries were unpinned: NATS-subject sanitization had never asserted the **whitespace family** explicitly (`\t` `\n` `\r` — a refactor hardcoding only `' '` would silently let the others through and fork the subject), nor the **wildcard family** (`*` `>` — letting these through would let an attacker craft a publish that overlaps a subscription it shouldn't, e.g. an adversarial filename of `*` matching `actions.google.*`), nor the **dot passthrough** (the subject hierarchy separator — filtering it would collapse multi-token actions into one token), nor the **ConnectError Debug** wire shape. On the policy-trace side, `set_layer` had been tested for replace + append separately but **never both at once with siblings present** (a `swap_remove` refactor would silently scramble the Vec order that `summary` renders against), the comma-and-Vec-order **summary join** contract was inferred but never asserted directly (alphabetical-sort regression: `layer_a` before `layer_b` even when LayerB landed first — the test below puts ReadFilter FIRST in the Vec so an alphabetical sort would surface), and **`emit` don't-panic** on allowed AND denied paths (a panic in the trace emitter would crash the worker mid-request) was unpinned. Files:

- [crates/proxy/src/forwarder/nats.rs](../../crates/proxy/src/forwarder/nats.rs) — 4 new tests. `sanitize_replaces_whitespace_tab_and_newline_with_underscore` pins `\t` `\n` `\r` + space → `_`. `sanitize_replaces_nats_wildcard_chars_star_and_gt` pins the `*` and `>` subject-wildcard collapse. `sanitize_dot_passthrough_preserves_subject_hierarchy` pins `.` AS the separator (passes through with leading/trailing variants). `connect_error_debug_includes_struct_name_for_grep` pins `ConnectError(...)` in the rendered Debug.
- [crates/proxy/src/adapters/policy_trace.rs](../../crates/proxy/src/adapters/policy_trace.rs) — 3 new tests. `set_layer_replaces_only_matching_layer_leaving_siblings_intact` pins in-place replacement against a 3-layer Vec (LayerB-failed replacement leaves LayerA + ReadFilter untouched at their original Vec indices). `summary_join_uses_comma_and_preserves_layer_order` pins the comma-separator + Vec-order rendering against an alphabetical-sort regression (`read_filter=ok,layer_a=ok` — ReadFilter first because LayerB-then-LayerA would also pass an alphabetical sort). `emit_does_not_panic_on_allowed_and_denied_traces` pins the don't-panic invariant on both INFO + WARN paths.

- [crates/proxy/src/notifier/mod.rs](../../crates/proxy/src/notifier/mod.rs) — 3 new tests. `approve_url_handles_trailing_slash_in_proxy_public_url` pins the current `https://proxy.example//api/v1/...` double-slash shape. `from_record_carries_multi_op_slice_through_serialize` pins a 3-element JSON array round-trip. `from_record_invariant_layer_round_trips_through_json` pins `layer = "invariant"` byte-stability on the wire.

- [crates/proxy/src/oauth/error.rs](../../crates/proxy/src/oauth/error.rs) — 3 new tests. `oauth_error_display_strings_pinned_for_log_filters` pins five `#[error(...)]` substrings. `upstream_variant_status_is_502_bad_gateway` constructs a real `reqwest::Error` via a 1ms timeout against `192.0.2.1:1` and pins both the 502 status and the `upstream_unavailable` body code. `into_response_500_branch_uses_internal_error_code_for_db_path` pins the Db→500→`internal_error` triple.

proxy bin tests: **521 → 527**.

**2026-05-17 (round 48) — 7 more pure-helper tests on oauth/routes + api/notifier_slack.** The 7 existing `routes` tests covered `pct` reserved-chars, `oauth_error_class` denied-vs-error on 9 variants, `intersect_scope_with_ops` happy + unknown-filter, `narrowed_ops_for_pca1` happy + empty, and `new_auth_code` shape. The 4 existing `notifier_slack` tests covered `header_str` ascii + non-ascii, `slack_ok_message` happy, and `slack_err` 401. But the **`pct` empty + multibyte** boundaries were unpinned (the URL builder calls `pct(&state)` from paths that can hand it empty input; the per-byte UTF-8 encoding is the wire-safety contract for `é` → `%C3%A9`), the **`oauth_error_class` Upstream/Db classification** (the `error` bucket — moving either into `denied` would bias the dashboard's "upstream is broken" vs "your config is broken" panels), the **`profile` always-keep branch** of `intersect_scope_with_ops` (the OIDC display-name surface — the existing tests pin `openid` + `email` but not `profile`), and the **empty-intersection** edge (no scope matches AND no always-keep — must be `""`, not panic, not fall back to a "safe default") were unpinned. On the Slack side, **case-insensitive header lookup** (Slack's Go SDK sometimes sends `X-Slack-Signature` mixed-case; axum's HeaderMap normalizes — pinning this catches a refactor that iterated raw bytes), **`slack_err` 500-path** (the rare DB-failure case; the helper accepts any status — a hardcoded 401 would silently downgrade), and the **`slack_ok_message("")` empty-text** boundary (no validation of input) were unpinned. Files:

- [crates/proxy/src/oauth/routes.rs](../../crates/proxy/src/oauth/routes.rs) — 4 new tests. `pct_handles_empty_and_multibyte_utf8_inputs` pins `""` → `""` + `é` → `%C3%A9`. `oauth_error_class_categorizes_upstream_and_db_as_error_bucket` pins Db → `error` directly. `intersect_scope_keeps_profile_alongside_openid_and_email` pins all three always-keep matches. `intersect_scope_collapses_to_empty_when_no_scope_matches_and_no_always_keep` pins the empty-intersection contract.
- [crates/proxy/src/api/notifier_slack.rs](../../crates/proxy/src/api/notifier_slack.rs) — 3 new tests. `header_str_is_case_insensitive_per_http_spec` pins the HeaderMap normalization. `slack_err_emits_500_for_internal_error_path` pins the helper accepting any status. `slack_ok_message_carries_empty_text_through_to_wire` pins the no-validation empty-text round-trip.

**2026-05-17 (round 49) — 7 more pure-helper tests on oauth/bridge + api/killswitch.** The 8 existing `bridge` tests covered the rejects-expired + 7 issuer classifications + malformed-JWT + bad-base64 + future-issued + iss round-trip + secondary-substrings + accepts-fresh. But the **clock-skew boundary** (`iat == now + 60` ACCEPTS, `iat == now + 61` REJECTS — a `>=` refactor would silently shrink the allowance by one second), the **`pca_0_cbor_b64` Option round-trip** (load-bearing `#[serde(default)]` — production bridges pre-fetch the bytes; stubs omit them), the **`FederationClaims::Clone`** field passthrough (the router clones into per-request state before persisting to the session row), and the **case-insensitive `infer_idp`** (the `to_ascii_lowercase` step is load-bearing — a metric-label split would appear for the same IdP) were unpinned. On the killswitch side, the **zero-`bearers_revoked` wire shape** (the dashboard "no-op" toast keys on `>= 0` — a refactor to `Option<i64>` would silently break it), the **forward-compat unknown-field acceptance** (no `deny_unknown_fields` — the CLI can add fields without breaking existing proxies), and the **`ApiError::BadRequest` Display verbatim shape** (the CLI's renderer parses the bare message — symmetric ApiError modules carry a `"bad request: {0}"` prefix, this one does not) were unpinned. Files:

- [crates/proxy/src/oauth/bridge.rs](../../crates/proxy/src/oauth/bridge.rs) — 4 new tests. `accepts_token_at_exact_60s_clock_skew_boundary` pins both sides (`+60` ok, `+61` rejected). `federation_claims_round_trip_pca_cbor_b64_optional` pins both Some(_) and absent shapes via two JWTs. `federation_claims_clone_preserves_every_field` pins every Clone-derived field. `infer_idp_case_insensitive_against_mixed_case_issuers` pins the three primary IdP buckets against mixed-case inputs.

proxy bin tests: **534 → 541**.

**2026-05-17 (round 50) — 6 more pure-helper tests on crypto/token_cipher + crypto/bearer.** The 9 existing `token_cipher` tests covered round-trip + distinct-nonces + wrong-key + short-key + tampered-CT + wrong-nonce-len + Ciphertext::Clone + BadKeyLen Display + empty-key. The 11 existing `bearer` tests covered round-trip format + wrong-prefix + lowercase-body + hash-stable + Debug-no-leak + length boundaries (51/53) + bad digits + as_bytes shape + Debug truncation + distinct generation + PartialEq distinguishing. But several boundaries the production hot path depends on were unpinned: the **empty-plaintext round-trip** (Google OAuth omits refresh_token → empty bytes still must round-trip cleanly via the 16-byte GCM tag), the **4 KB plaintext round-trip** (OAuth ID tokens hit this size; a fixed-size buffer refactor would surface here), the **`CipherError::Aead` Display** (operator log filter substring), the **empty/prefix-only/half-typed bearer parse** boundaries on the fast-path length check (auth middleware sometimes receives an empty header value), the **byte-for-byte parse-returns-input identity** (the SHA-256 hash MUST match the slice the parser was handed — any normalization would invalidate every persisted hash), and the **`BearerHash::Clone`** owning-array semantic (the killswitch path stashes a copy in the kill_cache). Files:

- [crates/proxy/src/crypto/token_cipher.rs](../../crates/proxy/src/crypto/token_cipher.rs) — 3 new tests. `empty_plaintext_round_trips_with_nonzero_ciphertext` pins empty PT + 16-byte tag. `large_plaintext_round_trips_through_encrypt_decrypt` pins a 4096-byte payload. `aead_error_display_is_stable_for_log_filters` pins the exact Display string `"AES-GCM operation failed"`.
- [crates/proxy/src/crypto/bearer.rs](../../crates/proxy/src/crypto/bearer.rs) — 3 new tests. `parse_rejects_empty_string_and_prefix_only` pins the three fast-fail length cases (`""`, `PREFIX`, prefix + 26 chars). `parse_returns_input_slice_byte_for_byte_when_valid` pins byte-for-byte identity. `bearer_hash_clone_yields_independent_array` pins the `[u8; 32]` Copy-via-Clone disjointness.

proxy bin tests: **541 → 547**.

**2026-05-17 (round 51) — 7 more pure-helper tests on policy-engine trace + decision.** The 10 existing `trace` tests covered the LayerOutcome::passed/failed shapes + 4 trace allowed/denied paths + OpsAtomView round-trip + PolicyLayer snake_case + PolicyEvalMode default + JSON null-not-omitted for None fields + trace JSON envelope. The 12 existing `decision` tests covered Pattern literal/regex + Decision serde 4 variants + QuarantineAction Copy + ReadFilter Clone + literal empty-haystack edge + decision kind wire snapshot. But several invariants the engine relies on were unpinned: the **empty-layers + Allow** vacuous-true (engine path always emits Layer A/B stubs but mocks construct empty traces), the **fail-at-third-layer** ordering invariant (`.all(passed)` must NOT short-circuit on the first pass — a single later-layer fail must flip allowed to false), the **duration_micros public-field mutation** (engine post-stamps the field after measuring eval time — a private-field refactor would force a builder method), the **OpsAtomView exact 3-key wire shape** (no extra fields; Trust Plane's parser is strict). On `decision`: the **PartialEq distinguishing Block variants by reason AND override_allowed** (the dashboard's diff-view re-renders on inequality), the **RateLimit burst/per_seconds independent round-trip** (an accidental field-order swap during refactor would silently flip the rate-limit semantics — used distinct values 7 and 42), and the **Pattern::Regex Clone** preserving compiled state (a `Cow<'static, str>` + re-compile-on-match refactor would silently re-pay compile cost per request on the hot path). Files:

- [crates/policy-engine/src/trace.rs](../../crates/policy-engine/src/trace.rs) — 4 new tests. `allowed_returns_true_for_empty_layers_with_allow_decision` pins the vacuous-`all` case. `allowed_false_when_first_layer_passes_but_a_later_layer_fails` pins the must-check-every-layer invariant on a 3-layer trace. `duration_micros_starts_zero_and_supports_post_construction_mutation` pins both zero-on-construct + pub field. `ops_atom_view_serializes_with_all_three_fields_present` pins all three field names + zero extras.
- [crates/policy-engine/src/decision.rs](../../crates/policy-engine/src/decision.rs) — 3 new tests. `decision_partial_eq_distinguishes_block_variants_by_reason_and_override` pins all four equality/inequality permutations. `rate_limit_round_trips_burst_and_per_seconds_separately` pins both integer fields with distinct values. `regex_pattern_clone_preserves_compiled_state` pins the compiled-regex Clone semantic via positive + negative match.

**2026-05-17 (round 52) — 7 more pure-helper tests on auth_middleware + policy-engine/context.** The 10 existing `auth_middleware` tests covered RefreshCoordinator (same-hash + distinct-hash + empty-default) + verify_with_key (untampered + tampered + wrong-key) + AuthFail Display strings + PcaCacheMiss substring + AuthFail::Db `#[from]` + the 401 body byte-stable. The 10 existing `context` tests covered customer_domain + path/user.email + headers + body string/non-string + unknown-head + list-array + non-array + list-with-non-string + path-and-headers-never-list. But several invariants the hot path relies on were unpinned: the **mutex-independence of refresh locks** (a regression that collapsed all bearers to one mutex would deadlock on the second acquire — caught via try_lock while holding A), the **AuthFail::Decrypt no-leak** semantic (the variant has no inner field — pin same-string-across-calls so a refactor that wrapped CipherError surfaces here as a wire-shape change), and the **AuthState::Clone trait bound** (every middleware invocation clones it via axum's State<T>; a !Clone field added later would surface at hundreds of router-build sites rather than at this single trait-bound assertion). On `context`: **missing-key vs null-value distinction** (OpsExpression treats `None` as "fall through to bare template" but `Some("null")` would produce a literal `:null` atom on the wire — load-bearing distinction), **empty list `Some(vec![])`** vs `None` (the substitution path treats Some(empty) as zero atoms, not as fall-through), **`user.email` empty-string returns Some("") not None** (policy authors use `user.email == ""` as a sentinel — a `Option<String>` refactor would flip this), and the **customer_domain Clone-through** survival. Files:

- [crates/proxy/src/auth_middleware.rs](../../crates/proxy/src/auth_middleware.rs) — 3 new tests. `refresh_coordinator_distinct_hashes_produce_independent_locks` pins lock disjointness via try_lock-while-held. `auth_fail_decrypt_does_not_carry_cipher_internals_in_message` pins the fixed-string Display + same-call-twice equality. `auth_state_is_clone_for_axum_state_propagation` pins the Clone trait bound at compile time via `fn require_clone<T: Clone>()` (no instantiation needed).
- [crates/policy-engine/src/context.rs](../../crates/policy-engine/src/context.rs) — 4 new tests. `lookup_body_missing_key_returns_none_distinct_from_null_value` pins both cases of the load-bearing distinction. `lookup_list_empty_string_array_returns_empty_vec_not_none` pins `Some(empty)` against the `null vs []` substitution-path conflation. `lookup_user_email_with_empty_string_returns_some_empty` pins the sentinel-friendly `Some("")` shape. `lookup_customer_domain_carries_through_clone` pins the Clone-derived field passthrough.
