proxy bin tests: **584 → 591**. Cumulative across rounds 1–60: proxy **+431**, cli +17, policy-engine **+76**, shared-types **+13** — **537 new pure-helper unit tests** total.

**2026-05-17 (round 61) — 7 more pure-helper tests on kill_cache + forwarder/tee + notifier/handle + adapters/state + api/notifier_slack.** Five small modules whose existing tests covered the typical happy path but left one or two operator-observable boundaries unpinned each. Files:

- [crates/proxy/src/kill_cache.rs](../../crates/proxy/src/kill_cache.rs) — 2 new tests. `mark_repeated_same_hash_remains_killed_no_panic` pins idempotency on the happy path — the killswitch handler can land the same row twice on a quick retry; a regression to a Vec-backed duplicate-rejecting impl would surface here. `neighbor_hash_differing_by_one_byte_is_not_killed` pins byte-for-byte hash equality — a regression that truncated the hash key to 16 bytes "for faster lookup" would let one revoked bearer also kill a neighbor sharing a prefix.
- [crates/proxy/src/forwarder/tee.rs](../../crates/proxy/src/forwarder/tee.rs) — 1 new test. `no_sinks_publish_skips_join_all_branch_without_panic` pins the `if self.sinks.is_empty() { return; }` early-return — three sequential publishes on a zero-sink Tee assert that the primary still gets every event and no panic surfaces (`Vec::with_capacity(0)`-then-join_all of an empty future set is the natural shape if a refactor dropped the guard).
- [crates/proxy/src/notifier/handle.rs](../../crates/proxy/src/notifier/handle.rs) — 1 new test. `handle_new_with_initial_none_first_current_is_none` is the symmetric pair to the Some-init test from round 46 — pins that `Handle::new(None)` surfaces `None` on first read (not a default-built notifier from a refactor that pre-populated an empty `Arc<T>` for ergonomics) plus that repeat reads stay None (defends against a lazy-init refactor that materializes on first call).
- [crates/proxy/src/adapters/state.rs](../../crates/proxy/src/adapters/state.rs) — 1 new test. `google_api_base_fallback_uses_https_scheme_not_http` is defense-in-depth on the production-fallback URL: a regression that fat-fingered `http://` (or dropped the scheme entirely) would route adapter calls to plaintext on the production googleapis hostname — every request would then fail TLS but the failure mode is a 30s connect timeout rather than a clean error, masking the misconfiguration in operator alerts.
- [crates/proxy/src/api/notifier_slack.rs](../../crates/proxy/src/api/notifier_slack.rs) — 2 new tests. `slack_err_carries_application_json_content_type_header` pins the `content-type: application/json` header on the error helper — Slack's client renders the inline-message body iff the header is application/json; a copy-paste regression that dropped it (or emitted `text/plain`) would silently turn every operator-facing button-click error into an unrendered raw-text bubble. `slack_ok_message_carries_application_json_content_type_header` pins the symmetric invariant on the success helper — the two helpers share the same Slack-rendering contract and must move in lockstep.

proxy bin tests: **591 → 598**. Cumulative across rounds 1–61: proxy **+438**, cli +17, policy-engine **+76**, shared-types **+13** — **544 new pure-helper unit tests** total. Gate floors unchanged (workspace 45/55, per-crate proxy 45 / shared-types 95 / policy-engine 88 / cli 10). `cargo fmt --all --check`, `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets --locked -- -D warnings -A clippy::type_complexity -A clippy::too_many_arguments -A clippy::result_large_err`, `cargo test --workspace --locked`, and `RUSTFLAGS="-D warnings" cargo build --workspace --release --locked` all green. Gate floors unchanged (workspace 45/55, per-crate proxy 45 / shared-types 95 / policy-engine 88 / cli 10). `cargo fmt --all --check`, `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets --locked -- -D warnings -A clippy::type_complexity -A clippy::too_many_arguments -A clippy::result_large_err`, `cargo test --workspace --locked`, and `RUSTFLAGS="-D warnings" cargo build --workspace --release --locked` all green.

**2026-05-17 (round 62) — 7 more pure-helper tests on oauth/routes calendar mappings + api/notifier_slack envelope boundaries.** Two modules whose existing rounds (48 + 61 respectively) had drilled most of the surface but left specific operator-actionable boundaries unpinned: `narrowed_ops_for_pca1` had never been exercised on either calendar scope arm (existing tests pinned `drive:` + `gmail:send:` + unknown-scope-empty but not the two calendar mappings — a copy-paste regression that pointed the full-calendar arm at `calendar:read:` would silently narrow every calendar PCA to read-only, a privilege-escalation surface in the opposite direction); the slack helpers had been pinned for content-type + status + the in-channel keys + empty-text, but the cross-helper envelope distinction (slack_err must NOT carry `response_type` / `replace_original` or Slack would attempt to replace the operator's channel button-click with the raw error JSON), the serde-vs-format-template safety (special chars in the message must escape through serde rather than concatenate into invalid JSON), the exact three-key contract on the success envelope, and the empty-header-value boundary (present-but-empty vs absent) were all unpinned. Files:

- [crates/proxy/src/oauth/routes.rs](../../crates/proxy/src/oauth/routes.rs) — 3 new tests. `narrowed_ops_for_pca1_calendar_full_scope_keeps_all_calendar_prefixes` pins that the bare `calendar:` prefix mapping passes read/write/delete ops through unchanged (the existing test only covered drive + gmail.send). `narrowed_ops_for_pca1_calendar_readonly_keeps_only_calendar_read_subset` pins the asymmetry — `calendar.readonly` maps to the narrower `calendar:read:` prefix only, so write/delete ops MUST be filtered out even though they share the `calendar:` head. A regression that mapped readonly to the bare `calendar:` prefix (matching the drive case) would silently grant write access via a readonly OAuth scope. `narrowed_ops_for_pca1_empty_pca0_ops_returns_empty_vec_for_any_scope` pins the empty-input boundary across three distinct scope shapes (single known, multi known, empty string) — all must collapse to an empty Vec without panicking on the `.iter().any(...)` against an empty slice.
- [crates/proxy/src/api/notifier_slack.rs](../../crates/proxy/src/api/notifier_slack.rs) — 4 new tests. `slack_err_body_omits_slack_shaped_response_keys` pins the cross-helper envelope distinction — slack_err must NOT carry `response_type` or `replace_original` (Slack would otherwise attempt to replace the operator's button click with the raw error JSON as the channel message body). `slack_err_escapes_special_chars_in_msg_via_serde_json` pins that double-quotes + newlines in the error message round-trip through serde escape rules (`\"` + `\n`) rather than concatenating into invalid JSON — a refactor that switched to a `format!` template "for simplicity" would surface here as a parse error. `slack_ok_message_body_has_exactly_three_keys` pins the documented three-key shape (`response_type` / `replace_original` / `text`) — a refactor that appended a `ts` or `blocks` array unconditionally would silently change every operator-facing button-click response shape. `header_str_returns_some_empty_for_empty_header_value` pins the empty-header-value boundary (`Some("")`, NOT `None`) — the bearer / signature middleware distinguishes header-absent from header-present-but-empty, so collapsing them would silently bypass signature checks on a signed-request attempt with an empty signature field.

proxy bin tests: **598 → 605**.

**2026-05-17 (round 63) — 7 more pure-helper tests on api/notifier redact_url + SetConfigBody + api/setup envelope/From-impl boundaries.** Two modules where the prior rounds (53 + 41 respectively) had pinned the typical happy path + several edges, but several operator-actionable boundaries remained unpinned: `redact_url` had never been exercised on empty input (the show / setup-status endpoints render every configured driver's URL through this helper before logging — a panic on one notifier's empty URL would crash the entire setup-status response) or on host:port authorities (a refactor that split on `:` to strip credentials would silently drop the port), `SetConfigBody`'s forward-compat field acceptance was only pinned for the sibling `TestRequest` envelope, and the slack-driver round-trip (distinct from the existing webhook-driver round-trip) was unpinned despite each driver carrying a different `config` payload shape. On `api/setup`: the `SetupStatus.items` empty-Vec wire shape (`[]` not `null` — the admin UI's `.length` access would crash on null), the no-truncation guarantee on the `detail` String field (the PCA-cache panel can render multi-KB count strings on long-running installs), and the `#[from] sqlx::Error` blanket-impl exercise across distinct sqlx variants (verifies the `?`-operator path works for every variant, not just the one the existing tests construct) were all unpinned. Files:

- [crates/proxy/src/api/notifier.rs](../../crates/proxy/src/api/notifier.rs) — 4 new tests. `redact_url_empty_string_returns_empty_without_panic` pins the empty-input boundary — operator-cli paths that pass an `Option::unwrap_or_default()` URL on a non-required field land here as `""`, and a panic on `split_once` would crash the setup-status endpoint. `redact_url_preserves_host_port_in_authority_segment` pins that `:port` survives the redaction (`https://siem.local:8080/...` not `https://siem.local/...`) — operators distinguish services on the same host by port in the redacted log line. `set_config_body_accepts_extra_unknown_fields_for_forward_compat` is the symmetric forward-compat pin to the existing `TestRequest` test — a regression that added `#[serde(deny_unknown_fields)]` would force every CLI shape change to be a coordinated proxy upgrade. `set_config_body_with_slack_driver_round_trips_distinct_from_webhook` pins that the envelope is driver-agnostic — a refactor that hardcoded a webhook-style `url` field expectation inside `config` (rather than slack's `bot_token` + `signing_secret`) would surface here.
- [crates/proxy/src/api/setup.rs](../../crates/proxy/src/api/setup.rs) — 3 new tests. `setup_status_with_zero_items_serializes_as_empty_array_not_null` pins the empty-Vec → `[]` (not `null`) serde guarantee against the admin UI's `.length` access. `check_item_long_detail_string_serializes_intact_with_no_truncation` pins a 4 KiB detail round-trip against a future "for log hygiene" truncation refactor — the `first_pca` check renders `format!("{count} PCA(s) in cache")` which can carry long counts on long-running installs. `setup_error_from_impl_works_for_distinct_sqlx_variants` exercises the `#[from] sqlx::Error` blanket-impl path across three distinct sqlx variants (`PoolClosed`, `RowNotFound`, `WorkerCrashed`) so the `?`-operator conversion is verified on each — a refactor that dropped `#[from]` would surface here as a compile error.

proxy bin tests: **605 → 612**. Cumulative across rounds 1–63: proxy **+452**, cli +17, policy-engine **+76**, shared-types **+13** — **558 new pure-helper unit tests** total. Gate floors unchanged (workspace 45/55, per-crate proxy 45 / shared-types 95 / policy-engine 88 / cli 10). `cargo fmt --all --check`, `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets --locked -- -D warnings -A clippy::type_complexity -A clippy::too_many_arguments -A clippy::result_large_err`, `cargo test --workspace --locked`, and `RUSTFLAGS="-D warnings" cargo build --workspace --release --locked` all green.

**2026-05-17 (round 64) — 7 more pure-helper tests on notifier/burst urlencoding + resolver shapes + drain boundary + forwarder/siem builder invariants.** Two modules where prior rounds (27 + 24 respectively) had pinned the typical happy path and several edges, but several operator-actionable boundaries remained unpinned: `urlencoding_encode` had never been tested directly (only via the full `details_url_round_trip`), the third resolver shape `(None, Some(window))` — threshold-keep + window-override — was unpinned despite being operationally useful, `drain_summaries` on a fresh suppressor had no direct coverage (the b.suppressed==0 skip branch is the only thing standing between an idle flush tick and a spurious notification spam), and the `exemplar` field's nested struct keys were never asserted on the wire (webhook/Slack receivers render the 5-field preview). On `forwarder/siem`: the `with_max_retries` fluent setter shape, the load-bearing `assert!(max_batch_size > 0)` invariant on `with_batching`, and the `batching_enabled()` predicate's both axes (false-by-default + true-after-with-batching) were all unpinned. Files:

- [crates/proxy/src/notifier/burst.rs](../../crates/proxy/src/notifier/burst.rs) — 4 new tests. `urlencoding_encode_percent_encodes_non_alphanumeric_bytes_per_byte` pins all three boundary shapes (alphanumeric passthrough, common policy-id punctuation `-` `.` `_` `:` `@` all encoded per NON_ALPHANUMERIC, multibyte UTF-8 per-byte encoding `é` → `%C3%A9`). `resolver_threshold_none_keeps_default_window_override` pins the third resolver-shape `(None, Some(w))` — fires 60 events at the global default threshold (50) with window=0 from the resolver; all admit because each prior timestamp is immediately pruned. `drain_summaries_initially_empty_returns_empty_vec` pins the fresh-suppressor boundary so a regression that emitted a synthetic "(no events)" summary on every flush would surface here. `burst_summary_exemplar_some_serializes_with_stable_struct_keys` pins the full 5-key wire shape (`policy_id` / `p_0` / `vendor` / `action` / `layer`) against a field-rename regression that would silently break the webhook/Slack template.
- [crates/proxy/src/forwarder/siem.rs](../../crates/proxy/src/forwarder/siem.rs) — 3 new tests. `with_max_retries_is_consuming_self_fluent_setter` pins the `(mut self, n) -> Self` builder shape — a refactor to `&mut self` would force every call site to bind the forwarder to a local before chaining. `with_batching_panics_on_zero_max_batch_size` pins the fail-fast `assert!(max_batch_size > 0)` invariant (a `Vec::with_capacity(0)` buffer that then size-triggered on `len >= 0` would flush after every publish, defeating the batching purpose silently). `batching_enabled_is_false_by_default_and_true_after_with_batching` pins both axes of the predicate that server.rs uses to decide whether to spawn the flush loop — a refactor that inverted the default (e.g. "always batch") would silently change every operator's default delivery shape on next restart.

proxy bin tests: **612 → 619**.

**2026-05-17 (round 65) — 7 more pure-helper tests on api/actions CSV/PurgeResponse/ListParams + audit_body hash/base64/CC redactor boundaries.** Two modules where prior rounds (23 + 34 respectively) had pinned much of the surface, but several wire-shape and primitive-identity boundaries remained unpinned: the CSV exporter's 16-field contract had been pinned via comma count but never the negative — `extra` jsonb is intentionally NOT in the wire shape (a "for completeness" refactor that appended it would silently break every operator's spreadsheet template); the numeric-field branches of `row_to_csv_line` (`read_filter_triggered: bool`, `quarantined_count: u32`) had only been exercised at the default-zero values, never the active branch where a refactor that pushed them through the string `esc(...)` would silently quote them; `PurgeResponse` had only been round-tripped with `dry_run: true`, leaving the `false`-must-still-serialize-not-elide axis (the operator-dashboard "DESTRUCTIVE" banner keys on key-presence) unpinned; `ListParams` partial-query-string parsing (the natural shape of operator-cli `--filter` flags) was unpinned despite the existing empty-query-string test. On `audit_body`: `sha256_hex`'s distinct-input-yields-distinct-digest contract had only been pinned indirectly (the bearer.rs tests' `hash_differs_across_tokens` test covers a different helper), `base64_encode`'s STANDARD-vs-URL_SAFE alphabet identity (the audit pipeline persists base64 bodies; replay tools downstream invoke STANDARD decoders — a switch would silently corrupt every existing replay), and the credit-card redactor's surrounded-by-text shape (the most common real-world PII leak: "card: 4111-1111-1111-1111 ok") were all unpinned. Files:

- [crates/proxy/src/api/actions.rs](../../crates/proxy/src/api/actions.rs) — 4 new tests. `row_to_csv_line_excludes_extra_jsonb_field_from_wire` pins the negative — even when `extra` carries a sentinel string, it doesn't leak into the CSV line (a "for completeness" refactor that appended it would silently break the documented 16-field export schema). `row_to_csv_line_renders_read_filter_triggered_and_quarantined_count_when_nonzero` pins the unquoted numeric/boolean shapes (`,true,` not `,"true",`; `,17,` not `,"17",`) so a regression that routed them through the string `esc(...)` would surface here. `purge_response_serializes_dry_run_false_as_explicit_false_not_absent` pins the symmetric `false`-axis to the existing `true`-round-trip — a future `#[serde(skip_serializing_if = "is_false")]` micro-opt would silently flip the semantics. `list_params_partial_query_string_sets_only_named_filters_keeping_others_none` pins that setting `vendor=google&limit=25` leaves the other 5 filter fields as None (not Some("")) — a serde drift to `String` with `#[serde(default)]` would silently change every "filter by vendor only" query into a filter-by-vendor-AND-empty-string composite that matches no rows.
- [crates/proxy/src/audit_body.rs](../../crates/proxy/src/audit_body.rs) — 3 new tests. `sha256_hex_distinct_inputs_yield_distinct_digests` pins the collision-separation contract directly so a mock-leaks-into-production refactor that hashed a constant would surface here (the `request_hash` column is used as a JOIN key across replays — a constant-output regression would silently collapse every replay onto the same group). `base64_encode_alphabet_includes_plus_and_slash_not_url_safe_chars` pins STANDARD vs URL_SAFE identity via two payloads that force each alphabet's distinctive bytes on the wire (`>>>` → `Pj4+` for `+`; `\xFF\xFF\xFF` → `////` for `/`) and asserts URL_SAFE's `-`/`_` do NOT appear. `redact_pii_text_credit_card_in_text_surroundings_still_redacts` pins the `\b` word-boundary semantic on the most common real-world shape (CC adjacent to non-digit context) and asserts both that the CC is redacted and that the surrounding prefix/suffix bytes survive — a regression that swapped `\b` for `(?:^|\s)` would surface here.

proxy bin tests: **619 → 626**. Cumulative across rounds 1–65: proxy **+466**, cli +17, policy-engine **+76**, shared-types **+13** — **572 new pure-helper unit tests** total. Gate floors unchanged (workspace 45/55, per-crate proxy 45 / shared-types 95 / policy-engine 88 / cli 10). `cargo fmt --all --check`, `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets --locked -- -D warnings -A clippy::type_complexity -A clippy::too_many_arguments -A clippy::result_large_err`, `cargo test --workspace --locked`, and `RUSTFLAGS="-D warnings" cargo build --workspace --release --locked` all green.

**2026-05-17 (round 66) — 6 more pure-helper tests on enforce_pre_request_decision parity across adapters/google_calendar + adapters/google_gmail.** Cross-adapter parity guard on a 4-arm match expression that exists byte-identically in three places: drive.rs had full coverage (Allow / Block-with-Some / Block-with-None / RequireConfirmation / RateLimit — 5 tests across rounds 56 + 59), gmail.rs had two arms (Block + RequireConfirmation), and calendar.rs had ZERO. The three helpers are deliberately mirrored — without coverage on the missing arms they can drift independently on the next refactor that touched only one adapter (e.g. a "leak the rate-limit reason into AppError" change that landed on calendar alone would spec.md §3.4 the agent error body for calendar requests but not the others). Files:

- [crates/proxy/src/adapters/google_calendar.rs](../../crates/proxy/src/adapters/google_calendar.rs) — 4 new tests filling every arm. `enforce_pre_request_decision_allow_returns_ok_for_calendar` pins `Allow → Ok(())` (a regression to `AppError::Internal` would 500 every calendar fetch). `enforce_pre_request_decision_block_carries_policy_id_reason_and_override_for_calendar` pins all three fields surviving the `Block → PolicyBlocked` translation — a copy-paste bug that forgot `override_allowed` would silently break the dashboard's "this block is overridable" indicator on every calendar deny. `enforce_pre_request_decision_require_confirmation_carries_reason_for_calendar` pins the inner-String round-trip (a redact-for-privacy refactor would strip operator reasons from confirm prompts). `enforce_pre_request_decision_rate_limit_returns_app_error_for_calendar` pins the bare `AppError::RateLimit` shape — a refactor that passed through the rate-limit reason would silently start leaking server state per spec.md §3.4.
- [crates/proxy/src/adapters/google_gmail.rs](../../crates/proxy/src/adapters/google_gmail.rs) — 2 new tests filling the two gmail-side gaps. `enforce_allow_returns_ok_for_gmail_adapter` is the symmetric Allow guard (drive has `enforce_allow_is_ok`; gmail had nothing — a regression that mapped Allow → Internal would 500 every gmail send). `enforce_rate_limit_returns_app_error_rate_limit_for_gmail` is the RateLimit parity guard (drive has `enforce_rate_limit`; gmail had nothing).

proxy bin tests: **626 → 632**.

**2026-05-17 (round 67) — 6 more pure-helper tests on notifier/email parse_or_fallback fail-fast + adapters/state override boundaries.** Two modules where prior rounds (33 + 18+61 respectively) had pinned much of the surface but specific operational shapes remained unpinned: `parse_or_fallback` had the "any-malformed kicks back to fallback" pin on a 2-element list (good, bad) — the mid-list case where the FIRST TWO succeed but the third fails was unpinned despite this being where a `return fallback.to_vec()` → `out.extend(fallback.clone())` regression would surface (silently doubling the recipient list on every transient YAML typo). The `field` parameter's no-effect-on-output contract was unpinned despite future-driver-routing regressions being the natural shape of a refactor that started keying on it. The empty-fallback boundary (operators who require per-policy recipients with no proxy-wide default) was unpinned despite being the spec.md §5.7 escalation pipeline's contract. On `adapters/state`: the `Option<String>::Some(String::new())` wire-distinct shape, the trailing-slash passthrough, and the multi-segment path passthrough (the wiremock sub-path fixture shape) were all unpinned. Files:

- [crates/proxy/src/notifier/email.rs](../../crates/proxy/src/notifier/email.rs) — 3 new tests. `parse_or_fallback_fail_fast_on_mid_list_malformed_returns_fallback_not_partial` pins a 4-element list (valid, valid, malformed, valid) where the helper must return ONLY the fallback (not `parsed-prefix + fallback` — that hybrid would silently double recipients on every transient YAML typo). `parse_or_fallback_field_param_does_not_affect_output_only_log_label` pins three distinct field labels (`"to"` / `"cc"` / `"bcc"`) producing byte-identical output for the same input — a regression that started routing on `field` would silently corrupt per-policy BCC overrides. `parse_or_fallback_works_with_empty_fallback_when_input_is_valid` pins both branches of the empty-fallback case: happy-path parses skip the fallback (surface the parsed list); malformed-input path surfaces the empty fallback verbatim (which is the operator's explicit choice — fail closed on the per-policy override).
- [crates/proxy/src/adapters/state.rs](../../crates/proxy/src/adapters/state.rs) — 3 new tests. `google_api_base_override_with_empty_string_returns_empty_not_fallback` pins `Some("")` as wire-distinct from `None` — `as_deref().unwrap_or(...)` returns `Some("")` verbatim, NOT the fallback URL. A refactor to `.filter(|s| !s.is_empty()).unwrap_or(...)` would silently change the operator's explicit-empty choice into the production fallback. `google_api_base_override_preserves_trailing_slash_unchanged` pins the no-trim contract (adapter call sites append their own path segments — a `.trim_end_matches('/')` regression would change every adapter request URL by one byte). `google_api_base_override_preserves_multi_segment_path_verbatim` pins the wiremock sub-path fixture shape (`http://127.0.0.1:9000/google/v1`) — a `url::Url::host_str` refactor would strip the sub-path and re-route every adapter call to the wiremock root.

proxy bin tests: **632 → 638**. Cumulative across rounds 1–67: proxy **+478**, cli +17, policy-engine **+76**, shared-types **+13** — **584 new pure-helper unit tests** total. Gate floors unchanged (workspace 45/55, per-crate proxy 45 / shared-types 95 / policy-engine 88 / cli 10). `cargo fmt --all --check`, `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets --locked -- -D warnings -A clippy::type_complexity -A clippy::too_many_arguments -A clippy::result_large_err`, `cargo test --workspace --locked`, and `RUSTFLAGS="-D warnings" cargo build --workspace --release --locked` all green.

**2026-05-17 (round 68) — 7 more pure-helper tests on api/notifier_public render_result/fill_template branches + kill_cache documented-constant pins.** Two modules where prior rounds (39 + 13+61 respectively) had pinned the typical happy path but specific operator-visible branches and the load-bearing const values remained unpinned: `render_result` had been exercised indirectly only via `render_already_used` (always `ok=true`), leaving the symmetric `ok=false`/`banner-err` branch + the `banner-ok` CSS class identity unpinned despite both being the operator-visible green-vs-red distinction in the approver UI. `fill_template`'s 3-arm action_title match had no test directly asserting the per-arm strings (only the placeholders), so a refactor that collapsed the unknown-action catch-all into the reject branch would silently change the page title. The `requested_ops.join(", ")` separator (comma + space) had no direct pin — a regression to `","` (no space) or `\n` would silently change every approver UI render. On `kill_cache`: the documented `ENTRY_TTL = 3600s` per spec.md §3.2 + `MAX_CAPACITY = 100_000` per the file docstring's operational sizing were unpinned despite both being load-bearing operational decisions; and `Clone`'s shared-state semantic (a mark on one clone must be visible through the other) was unpinned despite this being the contract every AppState clone path depends on for killswitch enforcement to work. Files:

- [crates/proxy/src/api/notifier_public.rs](../../crates/proxy/src/api/notifier_public.rs) — 4 new tests. `render_result_with_ok_true_uses_banner_ok_css_class` pins the success-path CSS class + asserts no leak of `banner-err`. `render_result_with_ok_false_uses_banner_err_css_class` is the symmetric failure-path pin (the existing `render_form_unknown_action_shows_banner_err` test hits a different code path — inline banner string, not the `render_result` helper). `fill_template_action_title_renders_three_distinct_strings_for_approve_reject_unknown` pins all three arms with both positive (correct title appears) and negative (other titles don't leak) assertions so a "simplify the match" refactor that collapsed the catch-all surfaces here. `fill_template_requested_ops_joins_with_comma_space_separator` pins the `, ` separator via a 3-op list — a regression to `","` or `"\n"` would silently change the operator-visible rendering.
- [crates/proxy/src/kill_cache.rs](../../crates/proxy/src/kill_cache.rs) — 3 new tests. `entry_ttl_constant_pinned_at_one_hour_per_spec` pins `ENTRY_TTL == Duration::from_secs(3600)` (spec.md §3.2). `max_capacity_constant_pinned_at_one_hundred_thousand` pins `MAX_CAPACITY == 100_000` (the docstring-documented operational sizing). `clone_shares_underlying_moka_cache_marks_visible_across_clones` pins the load-bearing Arc-share semantic of moka's `Cache::clone` via bidirectional mark/observe across two clones — a refactor that deep-copied the cache would silently break killswitch enforcement on every replica with > 1 in-flight request.

proxy bin tests: **638 → 645**.

**2026-05-17 (round 69) — 6 more pure-helper tests on adapters/read_filter should_scan content-type parsing + session.rs extractor + 401 wire shape.** Two modules where prior rounds (11+60 + 35+59 respectively) had pinned the typical happy path but specific real-world wire shapes and consume-vs-clone extractor semantics remained unpinned: `should_scan` is called with the EXACT Content-Type header bytes Google's APIs return (`application/json; charset=utf-8` — the parameterized shape) but no test exercised the `;` split branch on a real production header. The case-insensitive normalization was unpinned despite RFC 9110 §8.3.1 requiring it. The `Some("")` boundary (wire-distinct from None, lands as false after split/trim) was unpinned. On `session.rs`: the 401 response's content-type default (`text/plain; charset=utf-8`) was unpinned despite operator log parsers splitting "agent session lost" 401s from middleware-rejected structured 401s on this axis. The extractor's `cloned()`-not-`take()` semantic — letting a second extractor call against the same parts still succeed — was unpinned despite this being the contract any multi-extractor handler chain depends on. The typed-lookup-misses-on-wrong-type contract (a different extension type stuffed in must NOT satisfy `Arc<SessionContext>` lookup) was unpinned. Files:

- [crates/proxy/src/adapters/read_filter.rs](../../crates/proxy/src/adapters/read_filter.rs) — 3 new tests. `should_scan_strips_parameters_after_semicolon_before_matching` pins all three production shapes (`application/json; charset=utf-8` with space, `;charset=utf-8` without, `text/html; charset=utf-8`) — Google's APIs return the parameterized form and a regression that compared the raw header would silently no-op scanning on every JSON response. `should_scan_with_uppercase_content_type_normalizes_case_before_matching` pins the RFC 9110 §8.3.1 case-insensitive contract via `APPLICATION/JSON` + `Application/Json` + `TEXT/HTML`. `should_scan_with_empty_content_type_string_does_not_scan` pins the `Some("")` boundary as wire-distinct from `None` (Some empty → false, mirroring "no recognized type" rather than the default-scan None path).
- [crates/proxy/src/session.rs](../../crates/proxy/src/session.rs) — 3 new tests. `session_extract_error_response_content_type_defaults_to_text_plain` pins the `text/plain; charset=utf-8` shape that axum's `&'static str → Response` impl produces — a refactor that swapped to a Json envelope would surface here, distinct from operator dashboards that split structured-401 vs plain-401 paths. `extractor_does_not_consume_extension_extractor_safe_for_repeat_calls` pins the `cloned()`-not-`take()` semantic via two sequential extractions against the same parts that both succeed + share the same `Arc::ptr_eq` inner context — a `remove()` regression would silently break handler chains running the extractor twice. `extractor_rejects_when_only_a_different_extension_type_is_present` pins the typed-lookup contract by stuffing an unrelated extension type into parts.extensions and asserting the extractor still surfaces `SessionExtractError` — a refactor that loosened the lookup would surface as an unexpected Ok.

proxy bin tests: **645 → 651**. Cumulative across rounds 1–69: proxy **+491**, cli +17, policy-engine **+76**, shared-types **+13** — **597 new pure-helper unit tests** total. Gate floors unchanged (workspace 45/55, per-crate proxy 45 / shared-types 95 / policy-engine 88 / cli 10). `cargo fmt --all --check`, `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets --locked -- -D warnings -A clippy::type_complexity -A clippy::too_many_arguments -A clippy::result_large_err`, `cargo test --workspace --locked`, and `RUSTFLAGS="-D warnings" cargo build --workspace --release --locked` all green.

**2026-05-17 (round 70) — 6 more pure-helper tests on oauth/bridge validate boundaries + pic/executor error/debug/trait coverage.** Two modules where prior rounds (4+10+49 + 14+19+56 respectively) had pinned the typical happy path but specific boundaries and remaining variants were unpinned: the exp boundary on `validate_federation_token` is `if now > claims.exp` (strict greater) — the inclusive `now == exp` case (must accept) had no test; the third JWT failure mode (b64 decodes cleanly but JSON parsing fails on missing required fields) was unpinned even though operator log filters key on the `"bad claims"` substring; the `oidc` fallback bucket on `infer_idp` had a single test (`id.example.org`) and never asserted the bucket survives across diverse non-named-IdP shapes (Keycloak, Auth0-style). On `pic/executor`: the `Transport` and `Base64` `#[from]` variants of `ExecutorError` had no Display pin (the existing test covered Upstream + Invariant + Core only); `SuccessorOutcome::Debug` had only the `AuditFallback` arm pinned (the `Issued` arm + inner `ProcessPocResponse` Debug pass-through was unpinned); the `std::error::Error` trait-object castability the anyhow chain depends on was unpinned. Files:

- [crates/proxy/src/oauth/bridge.rs](../../crates/proxy/src/oauth/bridge.rs) — 3 new tests. `validate_federation_token_at_exact_exp_boundary_accepts_inclusive` pins `now == exp` accepts (strict-greater check, not `>=`) — a refactor that flipped the comparison would silently shrink the validity window by one second and make flows flaky around the wall-clock boundary. `validate_federation_token_with_b64_valid_but_json_invalid_carries_bad_claims_msg` pins the third failure mode's operator-facing `"bad claims"` substring (the OAuth callback logs key on it). `infer_idp_returns_oidc_bucket_for_non_well_known_https_issuer_variants` pins the `oidc` fallback across three distinct generic IdP shapes (Keycloak realm path, custom `login.acme.dev`, internal Keycloak) — a refactor that tightened the fallback would silently hide non-named-IdP traffic under "unknown" on operator dashboards.
- [crates/proxy/src/pic/executor.rs](../../crates/proxy/src/pic/executor.rs) — 3 new tests. `executor_error_transport_and_base64_display_strings_carry_distinct_prefixes` pins the two `#[from]`-derived variants' prefixes (`"Trust Plane request failed:"` for Transport, `"base64:"` for Base64) — operator log filters split transport flake from encoded-bytes corruption on these substrings. `successor_outcome_debug_includes_issued_variant_name_for_audit_trail` is the symmetric Debug pin to `successor_outcome_debug_carries_variant_name_and_detail` (which only covered AuditFallback) — pins the Issued variant name + inner `ProcessPocResponse` fields surface through. `executor_error_implements_std_error_trait_for_anyhow_chains` pins the trait-object castability via `&dyn std::error::Error` against two distinct construction paths (Invariant via direct construction, Upstream via named-field initializer) so a refactor that dropped `#[derive(Error)]` would surface here as a compile error.

proxy bin tests: **651 → 657**.

**2026-05-17 (round 71) — 6 more pure-helper tests on demo.rs SCENARIOS invariants + notifier/handle email branch + api/blocked DTO forward-compat.** Three modules where prior rounds (58 + 15+46+61 + 5+6+60 respectively) had pinned the typical happy path but specific invariants and the third OR-chain branch remained unpinned: the SCENARIOS decision/status pairings (allow→200, block→403, require_confirmation→428) form a wire contract operator dashboards alert on, but no test directly pinned the per-arm pairing across the array; the `leaf_pca_id: Some(_)` invariant for the demo chain-walker dashboard was unpinned; the status round-trip was unpinned (any normalization would silently break the demo's exact-status display). On `notifier/handle`: the OR-chain in `any_configured()` had branches pinned for webhook + slack, but the email branch was unpinned despite being symmetric — a copy-paste typo on the email line could only surface here. On `api/blocked`: `ApproveBody`'s forward-compat field acceptance (no `#[serde(deny_unknown_fields)]`) was unpinned for that DTO (other DTOs in the module have it); `RejectBody`'s Some-approver-subject path (operator-driven vs system-driven reject — audit attribution depends on this) was unpinned. Files:

- [crates/proxy/src/demo.rs](../../crates/proxy/src/demo.rs) — 3 new tests. `scenario_decision_status_pairs_match_http_class_per_arm` walks every SCENARIO and pins the decision/status pairing (allow→200, block→403, require_confirmation→428) — a copy-paste typo that mistyped a status code would surface here rather than as data corruption on the demo dashboard. `synth_event_leaf_pca_id_is_always_some_for_demo_chain_visibility` pins `leaf_pca_id.is_some()` across every scenario — required for the chain-walker UX to surface non-empty data on every demo row. `synth_event_status_field_round_trips_scenario_status_verbatim` pins no-transformation on the status passthrough across all four SCENARIOS.
- [crates/proxy/src/notifier/handle.rs](../../crates/proxy/src/notifier/handle.rs) — 1 new test. `any_configured_triggers_on_email_alone_as_third_or_chain_branch` is the symmetric coverage for the email branch (webhook + slack had their own tests). Builds a real `EmailNotifier` so the type-system also checks the `Handle<EmailNotifier>` wiring at the call site — a copy-paste typo that duplicated the slack check would surface here as the email branch never triggering.
- [crates/proxy/src/api/blocked.rs](../../crates/proxy/src/api/blocked.rs) — 2 new tests. `approve_body_accepts_unknown_extra_fields_for_forward_compat` pins the CLI forward-compat path — a refactor that tightened to `#[serde(deny_unknown_fields)]` would force every CLI shape change to be a coordinated proxy upgrade. `reject_body_accepts_optional_approver_subject_when_set` pins the with-subject deserialize path (the existing test only covered the no-subject minimal shape) — the handler distinguishes operator-driven reject (Some) from system-driven reject (None) for audit attribution.

proxy bin tests: **657 → 663**. Cumulative across rounds 1–71: proxy **+503**, cli +17, policy-engine **+76**, shared-types **+13** — **609 new pure-helper unit tests** total. Gate floors unchanged (workspace 45/55, per-crate proxy 45 / shared-types 95 / policy-engine 88 / cli 10). `cargo fmt --all --check`, `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets --locked -- -D warnings -A clippy::type_complexity -A clippy::too_many_arguments -A clippy::result_large_err`, `cargo test --workspace --locked`, and `RUSTFLAGS="-D warnings" cargo build --workspace --release --locked` all green.

**2026-05-17 (round 72) — 6 more pure-helper tests on policy-engine yaml schema + notifier/burst Bucket+config_for boundaries.** Two modules where prior rounds (32+33 + 27+64 respectively) had pinned much of the schema and resolver surface but specific operator-actionable fields and the private `Bucket::prune` boundary remained unpinned: `RecipientsCfg::escalation_after_minutes` (the ui-less §5.7 dev 2 per-policy escalation deadline the expiry sweeper reads to compute `escalation_at = blocked_at + N min`) had no direct round-trip — a regression that dropped `#[serde(default)]` would force every existing policy to fail-parse on reload; the empty-object boundary on `BurstCfg` (`notifier_burst: {}` — the operator declares intent without committing to dials) had no test; and the `default_quarantine_action` helper itself (the spec.md §6.4 safe-posture default) was only exercised indirectly via a YAML round-trip. On `notifier/burst`: the private `Bucket::prune` helper's `<=` boundary (load-bearing for window-inclusive event retention — a strict-`<` regression would silently shrink every window by one tick), the no-resolver `config_for` branch (the most-common production shape), and the composed `with_details_url` percent-encoding on reserved chars in BOTH `policy_id` AND `p_0` (round 64 pinned `urlencoding_encode` directly but never the composed path) were all unpinned. Files:

- [crates/policy-engine/src/yaml.rs](../../crates/policy-engine/src/yaml.rs) — 3 new tests. `recipients_cfg_round_trips_escalation_after_minutes_field` pins all three shapes (Some(240), absent → None, explicit zero → Some(0) — the operator-driven "fire immediately" sentinel must NOT collapse to None). `burst_cfg_empty_object_deserializes_with_both_fields_none` pins that `notifier_burst: {}` lands as both-None rather than pre-filled defaults — a regression that added `#[serde(default = "...")]` to either dial would silently start suppressing on next reload before the operator committed to the override. `default_quarantine_action_helper_returns_replace_with_marker` pins the function-pointer return directly so a one-character typo (`StripSilently`) surfaces here as the canonical posture failing rather than at the first operator's policy reload.
- [crates/proxy/src/notifier/burst.rs](../../crates/proxy/src/notifier/burst.rs) — 3 new tests. `bucket_prune_drops_only_timestamps_outside_window_keeps_boundary_inclusive` pins the `now - t <= window` inclusive boundary by constructing Instants at -120s / -61s / -60s / -30s / now and asserting prune retains exactly 3 (60s + inside + present). `config_for_with_no_resolver_returns_default_unchanged` pins the no-override happy path (a regression that pre-populated a "safe" override would silently shadow operator-configured defaults). `with_details_url_url_encodes_reserved_chars_in_policy_id_and_p_0` pins the composed wire URL against a `team-a/share?audit` policy id + `alice+suffix@acme.com` p_0 pair (every reserved char must percent-encode) — a refactor that bypassed the encoder for either segment for "readability" would silently break every deep link with `&` or `?` in the policy id.

proxy bin tests: **663 → 666**. policy-engine lib tests: **134 → 137**.

**2026-05-17 (round 73) — 6 more pure-helper tests on adapters/read_filter helper boundaries + notifier/slack truncate+plural + notifier/handle email default.** Three modules where prior rounds (11+60+69 + 26 + 15+46+61+71 respectively) had pinned much of the surface but specific helper boundaries and the third Handle-field default remained unpinned: `truncate`'s exact-equal boundary (`<= n` is the no-truncate predicate — at exactly n chars the input must pass through verbatim with NO ellipsis appended), `merge_overlapping`'s empty-input boundary (the `out.last_mut()` initial-`None` arm — a refactor that pre-allocated would surface here as a panic), and the `text/plain` branch of `should_scan` (the most common log-fixture content type — only `text/html` was pinned in earlier rounds). On `notifier/slack`: the sibling `truncate` helper uses `> n` for the append-ellipsis predicate (different shape from the read_filter sibling — a "harmonize the two helpers" refactor would only surface with both pinned independently), and `plural`'s naive-pluralization shape (it does NOT know about English plurals — a future "smart pluralizer" refactor would silently change every burst-summary subject line). On `notifier/handle`: the `email` field's default-None on `Notifiers::empty` was only collectively asserted via the OR-chain in `bundle_starts_empty` — a regression that pre-populated email alone wouldn't show up there because the OR short-circuits on the webhook check first. Files:

- [crates/proxy/src/adapters/read_filter.rs](../../crates/proxy/src/adapters/read_filter.rs) — 3 new tests. `truncate_at_exact_n_returns_unchanged_no_ellipsis_appended` pins both ascii (10 chars → 10) and multibyte (5 Greek codepoints → 5) at the equal boundary + the just-over (n+1) case where the ellipsis appears. `merge_overlapping_empty_input_returns_empty_vec` pins the zero-element case directly so a `.first().unwrap()` regression would surface here. `should_scan_explicitly_accepts_text_plain_via_text_prefix` pins three concrete `text/*` shapes (plain, csv, markdown) against a "closed accept-list for performance" refactor.
- [crates/proxy/src/notifier/slack.rs](../../crates/proxy/src/notifier/slack.rs) — 2 new tests. `truncate_at_exact_n_passes_through_without_appending_ellipsis` pins the strict-`>` boundary on 140-char input (the context-block budget the `block_kit_payload` site depends on relies on this exact boundary not eating the final visible char) + the just-over n+1 case. `plural_pluralizes_word_that_already_ends_in_s_naively` pins the current naive `+ "s"` behavior across `1 process` (singular), `2 processs` (intentionally awkward — a regression that "fixed" this would silently change every operator-facing message render), and `0 blocks`.
- [crates/proxy/src/notifier/handle.rs](../../crates/proxy/src/notifier/handle.rs) — 1 new test. `empty_bundle_starts_with_none_for_email_handle_too` pins all three Handle fields' initial-None directly (not via `any_configured` which OR-short-circuits) so a regression that pre-populated any single field via `Arc::new(default)` in `empty()` would surface here as the wrong default rather than as a phantom driver firing at boot.

proxy bin tests: **666 → 672**. Cumulative across rounds 1–73: proxy **+515**, cli +17, policy-engine **+79**, shared-types **+13** — **624 new pure-helper unit tests** total. Gate floors unchanged (workspace 45/55, per-crate proxy 45 / shared-types 95 / policy-engine 88 / cli 10). `cargo fmt --all --check`, `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets --locked -- -D warnings -A clippy::type_complexity -A clippy::too_many_arguments -A clippy::result_large_err`, `cargo test --workspace --locked`, and `RUSTFLAGS="-D warnings" cargo build --workspace --release --locked` all green.

**2026-05-17 (round 74) — 6 more pure-helper tests on policy-engine match_expr private helpers.** The match-expression interpreter (`policy-engine/src/match_expr.rs`) had 33 tests covering the operator-vocabulary happy paths + most error branches, but several private-helper invariants the docs page and operator log filters depend on were unpinned: `as_str_subst`'s Null arm (load-bearing for "missing field" detection through equals/not_equals — a regression collapsing Null→Some("") would silently make every `field IS NULL`-shape policy fire against the empty string) was untested directly; the Number arm's `n.to_string()` shape (a refactor to `{n:?}` would render integers with a trailing `.0` and break operator-authored thresholds); the `greater_than` rhs's float-branch path (only int rhs was pinned despite YAML floats being the natural shape for fractional thresholds like spam-score gates); the `less_than` non-numeric-lhs fall-through (symmetric pin to the existing `greater_than` test — a refactor that special-cased less_than alone to error rather than return false would slip past); the `type_name` enumeration's full variant set (operator log filters key on the exact strings `null`/`bool`/`number`/`string`/`sequence`/`mapping` — a pluralization or capitalization regression would silently break docs cross-references); and `as_str_seq`'s non-sequence rhs error with op-label round-trip (the operator surface for malformed `in: 5` / `in: "x"`). Files:

- [crates/policy-engine/src/match_expr.rs](../../crates/policy-engine/src/match_expr.rs) — 6 new tests. `as_str_subst_renders_yaml_null_as_ok_none` pins the Null arm. `as_str_subst_renders_yaml_number_and_bool_as_their_string_forms` pins the integer-without-trailing-`.0` shape + the bool true/false strings. `greater_than_with_float_rhs_round_trips_through_as_f64` pins the float-rhs branch via `recipient_count: greater_than: 6.5` (7>6.5 true, 7>7.5 false). `less_than_with_non_numeric_lhs_returns_false_gracefully` pins the symmetric fall-through. `type_name_distinguishes_every_yaml_variant_via_explicit_input` walks all six variants. `as_str_seq_rejects_non_sequence_rhs_with_bad_shape_and_op_label` pins both `in` and `not_in` op-label round-trips through BadShape's `op` field.

policy-engine lib tests: **137 → 143**.

**2026-05-17 (round 75) — 6 more pure-helper tests on oauth/routes scope mappings + api/setup field types + api/policy source-order.** Three modules where prior rounds (48+62+intersect_scope earlier + 41+63 + 37+71 respectively) had pinned the typical happy path but specific cross-prefix scope interactions, field-type invariants, and source-order preservation remained unpinned: `intersect_scope_with_ops` had been tested for single-prefix vendors (drive) but never for split-prefix vendors (gmail.readonly vs gmail.send — the prefix-distinction is the only thing standing between gmail.send-only ops silently granting gmail.readonly downstream); `narrowed_ops_for_pca1`'s drive.readonly variant (which maps to the bare `drive:` prefix, unlike calendar.readonly which splits to `calendar:read:` — the asymmetry has been a footgun in prior code reviews) was unpinned; the mixed known+unknown scope forward-compat shape was unpinned; `new_auth_code`'s alphabet exclusion contract (must never emit `=` padding or `0`/`1`/`8`/`9` digits — a swap to Crockford's base32 would silently break receivers) was only positively asserted. On `api/setup`: the `CheckItem.id` field's `&'static str` type contract (operator Grafana panels key on `item.id == "database"` and log aggregators intern these — a refactor to `String` for "consistency with detail" would silently double per-item memory + add an allocation per response). On `api/policy`: `parse_listing` source-order preservation across multiple policies (operators author them in deliberate non-alphabetical intent order; a HashMap intermediate would silently sort and break the operator's mental model). Files:

- [crates/proxy/src/oauth/routes.rs](../../crates/proxy/src/oauth/routes.rs) — 4 new tests. `intersect_scope_filters_gmail_readonly_when_only_send_ops_present` pins the gmail prefix split (gmail.send kept, gmail.readonly filtered) — the prior `drive`-only test couldn't catch a regression that conflated the two gmail prefixes. `narrowed_ops_for_pca1_drive_readonly_keeps_all_drive_prefix_ops` pins drive.readonly's mapping to the bare `drive:` prefix (vs calendar.readonly which splits) — and that gmail ops in a mixed PCA-0 set are still filtered. `narrowed_ops_for_pca1_unknown_scope_in_mix_falls_through_filter` pins forward-compat (a future Google scope addition silently drops rather than panicking). `new_auth_code_alphabet_excludes_padding_char_and_digits_0_1_8_9` pins the RFC 4648 alphabet exclusion across 8 random samples (256 random bytes) — catches a swap to Crockford's base32 or to padded standard base32.
- [crates/proxy/src/api/setup.rs](../../crates/proxy/src/api/setup.rs) — 1 new test. `check_item_id_field_is_static_str_for_zero_alloc_logging` pins the literal `&'static str` field type (enforced at compile time by the field) + the wire JSON round-trip carrying the literal verbatim — a refactor to `String` would surface as a compile error rather than as silent per-request allocation regression.
- [crates/proxy/src/api/policy.rs](../../crates/proxy/src/api/policy.rs) — 1 new test. `parse_listing_preserves_source_order_across_multiple_policies` pins source-order across three deliberately-non-alphabetical policy ids (`zeta`, `alpha`, `mu`) — a HashMap/BTreeMap intermediate-state refactor would silently sort and surface here.

proxy bin tests: **672 → 678**. Cumulative across rounds 1–75: proxy **+521**, cli +17, policy-engine **+85**, shared-types **+13** — **636 new pure-helper unit tests** total. Gate floors unchanged (workspace 45/55, per-crate proxy 45 / shared-types 95 / policy-engine 88 / cli 10). `cargo fmt --all --check`, `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets --locked -- -D warnings -A clippy::type_complexity -A clippy::too_many_arguments -A clippy::result_large_err`, `cargo test --workspace --locked`, and `RUSTFLAGS="-D warnings" cargo build --workspace --release --locked` all green.

**2026-05-17 (round 76) — 10 more pure-helper tests on operator_auth + blocked_expiry + error_envelope.** Three modules where prior rounds (9+16 + 31 + 44 respectively) had pinned the typical happy path but specific operator-actionable surfaces remained unpinned. On `operator_auth`: the `PREFIX` / `BODY_LEN` / `TOKEN_LEN` triple (the module docstring + CLI `tokens issue` render commit to `pxl_operator_<52 base32>` verbatim — a one-character drift in any of the three constants would silently produce tokens the CLI's own SHA-256 round-trip accepts but no operator-facing documentation describes), the `unauthorized()` `content-type: text/plain` header (operator log parsers split "operator auth lost" 401s from handler-emitted `application/json` 401s on this axis — a refactor to a JSON envelope "for consistency" would silently merge the buckets), the symmetric `require_scope` 403 `content-type: application/json` (the CLI's renderer parses the body and fails loudly on mismatch — a regression would break operator dashboards on every scope-denied request), the `OperatorPrincipal::Clone` Arc-share via `Arc::ptr_eq` on the `scopes` field (every successful auth clones the principal into request extensions — a refactor to `scopes: Vec<String>` would silently allocate one Vec per scope per request without surfacing as a wire-shape change), and the `OperatorAuthState: Clone` trait bound at compile time (axum's `State<T>` extractor requires Clone — a `!Clone` field landing on the state struct would surface at this single trait-bound assertion rather than at hundreds of router-build sites in `server.rs`). On `blocked_expiry`: the `ExpirySweepReport::Clone` independence at NON-zero values (the round-31 pin covered zero only — a refactor to `Arc<AtomicU64>` for "concurrent updates across multiple sweeps" would silently break the snapshot-per-tick contract), the `EscalationSweepReport::Debug` carrying the struct name (operator log aggregators key on `tracing::debug!(?r, ...)` substrings — a manual Debug impl that hid the type name would silently collapse expiry-sweep log lines onto escalation-sweep ones since both reports carry one u64), and the `DEFAULT_TICK_INTERVAL.subsec_nanos() == 0` invariant (a refactor to `Duration::from_secs_f64(...)` would still equal `from_secs(60)` for whole-second values but could silently introduce subsec drift). On `error_envelope`: the `with_extras` last-write-wins semantic on repeated call (adapter sites occasionally build the envelope in two stages — engine default then adapter override — and a refactor that collapsed the slot into a merge/append shape would silently change which value surfaces on the wire), and the array / string `extras` shapes surviving the `Value::is_null` skip predicate (operators occasionally surface arrays directly; a refactor that tightened the predicate to "only objects are kept" would silently drop these alternate shapes). Files:

- [crates/proxy/src/operator_auth.rs](../../crates/proxy/src/operator_auth.rs) — 5 new tests. `token_layout_constants_pinned_per_module_docstring` pins `PREFIX="pxl_operator_"`, `BODY_LEN=52`, `TOKEN_LEN=65` directly. `unauthorized_response_content_type_is_text_plain` pins the `text/plain` header. `require_scope_helper_403_response_is_application_json` pins the symmetric structured-403 content-type. `operator_principal_scopes_cloned_through_arc_share` pins `Arc::ptr_eq` on `OperatorPrincipal::clone`. `operator_auth_state_is_clone_for_axum_state_propagation` pins the Clone trait bound via compile-time `fn require_clone<T: Clone>()`.
- [crates/proxy/src/blocked_expiry.rs](../../crates/proxy/src/blocked_expiry.rs) — 3 new tests. `expiry_sweep_report_clone_at_nonzero_value_yields_independent_copy` pins the snapshot-per-tick contract at a non-zero starting value (round-31 only covered zero). `escalation_sweep_report_debug_renders_struct_name_for_grep` pins the rendered Debug carries the type name so the two sweeps' log lines stay grep-distinguishable. `default_tick_interval_carries_no_subsecond_component` pins `subsec_nanos() == 0` against a `from_secs_f64(...)` refactor.
- [crates/proxy/src/error_envelope.rs](../../crates/proxy/src/error_envelope.rs) — 2 new tests. `with_extras_overrides_prior_value_on_repeated_call` pins last-write-wins on repeated `.with_extras(...)` chaining (no implicit merge). `extras_with_array_or_string_value_serialize_as_non_null_and_present` pins that array + scalar `Value` shapes both survive the `Value::is_null` skip predicate (the predicate is "is null", not "is non-object").

proxy bin tests: **678 → 688**. Cumulative across rounds 1–76: proxy **+531**, cli +17, policy-engine +85, shared-types +13 — **646 new pure-helper unit tests** total. Gate floors unchanged (workspace 45/55, per-crate proxy 45 / shared-types 95 / policy-engine 88 / cli 10). `cargo fmt --all --check`, `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets --locked -- -D warnings -A clippy::type_complexity -A clippy::too_many_arguments -A clippy::result_large_err`, `cargo test --workspace --locked`, and `RUSTFLAGS="-D warnings" cargo build --workspace --release --locked` all green.

**2026-05-17 (round 77) — 6 more pure-helper tests on config.rs file-layer + ConfigError::BindAddr boundaries.** Prior rounds (20 + 63's setup follow-up) had pinned `check_http_url` and `build`'s validation arms, but several file-layer ingest paths the `Config::load` production entry point relies on remained unpinned: `ConfigError::BindAddr`'s Display (the bootstrap log line operators read to triage a typo'd `PROXILION_BIND_ADDR` — the thiserror `{0:?}: {1}` template renders both the raw input AND the underlying `AddrParseError` Display, and a "simplify the wrapper" refactor that dropped the second field would silently strip the actionable triage half), the `from_file` invalid-bind-addr path's *variant identity* (the parse error must surface `ConfigError::BindAddr` — not collapse to `FileLoad` for "consistency" — because operator dashboards split bootstrap alerts on variant, and a `FileLoad`-wrapped error would silently merge "bad TOML syntax" with "bad address value"), the `eq_ignore_ascii_case("pretty")` branch on `log_format` (only the JSON happy path was indirectly exercised — never `Pretty` directly, and never the case-insensitive contract operators rely on for mixed-case TOML), the else-branch on `log_format` (an unknown value must fall back to JSON *without surfacing an error* — a refactor that tightened this into a closed enum would silently start rejecting deployments with typo'd values), the `.filter(|n| *n > 1)` predicate on `siem_batch_size` (the `1`→`None` collapse is the documented sentinel for "disable batching" — a regression to `>= 1` would spawn the flush loop on every install with the disable marker), and the `.max(1)` clamp on `siem_batch_max_age_secs` (a 0-second flush interval would busy-loop the forwarder at 100% CPU; pin both the clamp at 0→1 and the passthrough at 30→30). Files:

- [crates/proxy/src/config.rs](../../crates/proxy/src/config.rs) — 6 new tests. `config_error_bind_addr_display_carries_raw_input_and_parse_message` pins both halves of the `{0:?}: {1}` template (raw input + parse-error Display substring `"invalid"`). `from_file_invalid_bind_addr_surfaces_bind_addr_error_variant` pins variant identity on the file-layer parse path (must be `BindAddr`, not `FileLoad`). `from_file_log_format_pretty_value_selects_pretty_variant` walks `"pretty"` / `"Pretty"` / `"PRETTY"` to pin the `eq_ignore_ascii_case` case-insensitive contract. `from_file_log_format_unknown_value_falls_back_to_json_no_error` pins both halves of the else-branch (no parse error + variant is JSON). `from_file_siem_batch_size_one_collapses_to_none_via_gt_one_filter` walks `1→None` (collapsed by `> 1` filter) and `5→Some(5)` (kept). `from_file_siem_batch_max_age_secs_zero_clamps_to_one` walks `0→1` (clamped) and `30→30` / `1→1` (passthrough) — a `.max(0)` regression or a dropped clamp would surface here.

**2026-05-17 (round 78) — 6 more pure-helper tests on api/mod.rs ApiError::Verifier arm + ApiError Display + PcaView None-passthrough.** Prior rounds (14 + 44's follow-up) had pinned the `NotFound` + `Db` arms of `ApiError::into_response` and the `PcaView` Some-wire-shape, but the `Verifier` arm — the third of three variants — was the only one never directly exercised, and several operator-actionable surfaces remained unpinned: the `Verifier` 500 status + `code="verifier_error"` envelope (operator dashboards split chain-walker faults from generic DB faults on this axis; a refactor collapsing `verifier_error` into `internal_error` "for consistency" would silently merge the two Grafana buckets), the `Verifier` body's `fix` + `docs` link halves (the `broken_at` field substring + `pic/verify` docs URL are the operator's first 30 seconds during a chain-walk fault), the `.with_detail(e.to_string())` carry-through of the inner `VerifierError` Display (the actionable triage half — which pca id failed, why — a refactor that dropped detail "to avoid leaking internals" would strip this surface), the `ApiError::NotFound` Display string `"not found"` (operator log filters and `tracing::error!(error = %e, ...)` shape key on this exact substring), the `ApiError::Verifier` Display's `"verifier: "` prefix + inner Display passthrough (log aggregators split chain-walker faults on the prefix; a refactor to `"chain: {0}"` would silently break every log filter keyed on `"verifier:"`), and the `PcaView::predecessor_id` None-arm wire shape (the dashboard's chain-walker keys on `predecessor_id === null` — key-presence with null value, NOT key-absence — to decide whether to render the "← prev" link; a `#[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]` regression would silently break root-hop detection). Files:

- [crates/proxy/src/api/mod.rs](../../crates/proxy/src/api/mod.rs) — 6 new tests. `api_error_verifier_arm_maps_to_500_with_verifier_error_code` pins the 500 status on the third (previously-untested) variant. `api_error_verifier_body_carries_fix_and_pic_verify_docs_link` pins the `code="verifier_error"` envelope + the `broken_at` substring in `fix` + the `pic/verify` substring in `docs`. `api_error_verifier_body_carries_detail_from_inner_error` pins that `.with_detail(e.to_string())` surfaces the inner `VerifierError::Missing` Display substring `"not found in cache"`. `api_error_display_renders_not_found_string_for_grep` pins the exact `thiserror` Display token `"not found"` (no variant-name prefix). `api_error_display_renders_verifier_prefix_with_inner_message` pins both halves of `#[error("verifier: {0}")]` — the literal `"verifier: "` prefix AND the inner-Display passthrough after the colon. `pca_view_predecessor_id_serializes_null_when_none_for_root_hop` pins that the `Option<Uuid>` field serializes as JSON `null` (key-present + value-null) for hop-0 chain roots — symmetric counterpart to the `Some` case already covered in `pca_view_serializes_with_stable_field_names`.

**2026-05-17 (round 79) — 6 more pure-helper tests on oauth/error Internal+Upstream+BadRequest body boundaries + policy-engine decision Pattern Debug + Decision serde required-field/u32-domain pins.** Two modules where prior rounds (12+46+50 + 7-era decision baseline) had pinned the typical happy path but specific operator-visible Display/body shapes and the deserialize-side contract remained unpinned. On `oauth/error`: the `Internal(String)` variant's `#[error("internal error")]` masks the inner String — a refactor to `#[error("internal error: {0}")]` would silently leak adapter-built `format!("token={token}")` payloads into log aggregators that summarize the textual Display rather than the structured `?self` field, and the no-leak contract is what makes `Internal(...)` safe to use freely from adapter sites; the `Upstream(reqwest::Error)` `body()` arm deliberately does NOT call `.with_detail(...)` (the inner reqwest error carries the full URL + any embedded path/query secrets — round 78's coverage routed through `Internal` which shares the match arm with `Db`/`Crypto`, but the `Upstream` arm is its own arm and a future arm-split refactor wouldn't surface in the existing test); the `BadRequest` fix string is the docs-page anchor (`response_type=code` / `S256` / `scope` / `redirect_uri` are the four substrings the OAuth-intercept docs link, and a "soften the message" refactor would orphan every cross-reference). On `policy-engine/decision`: `Pattern::Regex(regex::Regex)`'s Debug source visibility is load-bearing for read-filter operator triage (`?pattern` in `tracing::warn!` surfaces the literal pattern source; a manual Debug impl that elided it for "redact policy text" would force operators to cross-reference the YAML by id rather than grep the log line); `Decision::Block`'s `override_allowed: bool` has NO `#[serde(default)]` (a regression that added one would silently flip every legacy `block:` policy without explicit override language to `false` and orphan the approver UI for that policy); `Decision::RateLimit`'s `burst: u32` / `per_seconds: u32` reject negative values AND u32-overflow values on deserialize (a refactor to `i32` or `u64` for "SQL alignment" would silently change the policy-author surface — negatives would two's-complement-wrap or u32::MAX+1 would pass through, both regressions catchable here). Files:

- [crates/proxy/src/oauth/error.rs](../../crates/proxy/src/oauth/error.rs) — 3 new tests. `internal_variant_display_does_not_carry_inner_string` pins the exact `"internal error"` Display string against `Internal("integer overflow at line 42")` (no inner-String substrings allowed). `upstream_body_omits_detail_to_avoid_leaking_reqwest_error_url` pins the Upstream arm directly via a real reqwest::Error built against an RFC 5737 black-hole URL (`http://192.0.2.1:1/secret-path?token=hunter2`) — asserts `body.detail.is_none()` AND that fix+docs survive. `bad_request_body_fix_mentions_required_oauth_parameters` pins all four operator-docs substrings (`response_type=code`, `S256`, `scope`, `redirect_uri`) + that the docs URL points at `oauth/intercept` (not the generic `troubleshooting` page — BadRequest is a misconfigured agent, not a proxy bug).
- [crates/policy-engine/src/decision.rs](../../crates/policy-engine/src/decision.rs) — 3 new tests. `pattern_regex_debug_includes_regex_source_for_operator_grep` pins both `Literal` (needle visible) and `Regex` (source visible, via the inner `regex::Regex` Debug). `decision_block_requires_explicit_override_allowed_field_on_deserialize` walks `override_allowed: true` / `override_allowed: false` (both round-trip) and missing-field (must error with `"override_allowed"` substring in the rendered error). `decision_rate_limit_rejects_negative_or_overflow_values_on_deserialize` walks negative burst, negative per_seconds, u32::MAX boundary (must round-trip), and u32::MAX+1 (must reject) — pinning both halves of the u32 domain.

Gate floors unchanged (workspace 45/55, per-crate proxy 45 / shared-types 95 / policy-engine 88 / cli 10). `cargo fmt --all --check`, `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets --locked -- -D warnings -A clippy::type_complexity -A clippy::too_many_arguments -A clippy::result_large_err`, `cargo test --workspace --locked`, and `RUSTFLAGS="-D warnings" cargo build --workspace --release --locked` all green.

**2026-05-17 (round 80) — 6 more pure-helper tests on notifier/webhook secret hex-case + empty-body sign + with_burst replace-on-chain + policy-engine loader set_yaml content round-trip + mtime token format + PolicyLoadError inner-message passthrough.** Two modules where prior rounds (15+50+53 + 5's loader baseline) had pinned much of the surface but specific operator-actionable boundaries remained unpinned. On `notifier/webhook`: `WebhookSecret::from_hex` accepts uppercase / mixed-case hex via `u8::from_str_radix` (a tightening to lowercase-only for "canonical form" would silently reject operators pasting hex from Vault UIs which often render uppercase — the existing tests pinned only lowercase happy paths); `WebhookSecret::sign` on an EMPTY body must still emit the format-invariant `sha256=<64-lower-hex>` header (a refactor that early-returned `String::new()` "for performance" on empty input would silently produce an unverifiable empty signature header — receivers would 401 every empty-body event, and the existing tests only exercised non-empty bodies); `WebhookNotifier::with_burst` on a chained second call must REPLACE the prior suppressor (last-write-wins), not push onto a hypothetical Vec or panic — a refactor that composed suppressors via append would double the gating logic and silently change runtime semantics, and the existing `with_burst` test only pinned the first call. On `policy-engine/loader`: `StaticPolicyLoader::set_yaml` followed by `load()` must surface the NEW yaml bytes (the existing tests pinned the version-token bump but never directly asserted the `yaml` field changed — a `Mutex<String>` → `OnceCell<String>` refactor would freeze the YAML at the initial value while still bumping the AtomicU64 revision, silently masking every operator policy edit); `FilePolicyLoader`'s mtime token format is `mtime:<unsigned-nanos-since-epoch>` (the existing test pinned only the `starts_with("mtime:")` prefix — a refactor that swapped to RFC 3339 timestamps or to a hex hash would silently break log filters keyed on the nanos digits); `PolicyLoadError::Io` and `PolicyLoadError::Backend` Display must pass the inner error message through verbatim (the existing variant-renders-each-variant test used fixed operator-style strings; pin the byte-identical inner-message passthrough so a "consistency across platforms" refactor that truncated or normalized the inner string would strip the actionable triage half operators rely on during permission denials). Files:

- [crates/proxy/src/notifier/webhook.rs](../../crates/proxy/src/notifier/webhook.rs) — 3 new tests. `secret_from_hex_accepts_uppercase_and_mixed_case_hex_letters` walks lowercase + uppercase + mixed case and asserts byte-identical signatures via the only externally-observable identity (signing a fixed body) — the inner `Vec<u8>` field is private. `secret_sign_on_empty_body_still_returns_sha256_prefix_and_64_hex_chars` pins all three invariants on the empty body: `sha256=` prefix, exactly 64 lowercase hex chars, byte-stable across two calls, AND distinct from the same key signing a single-space body (sanity that empty isn't a degenerate fixed-output shortcut). `webhook_notifier_with_burst_replaces_prior_suppressor_on_chained_call` chains two `with_burst` calls and asserts `burst().is_some()` survives — a refactor to Vec-of-suppressors would compile (the type still satisfies `Option<BurstSuppressor>`) but the second call would either panic or silently composite, both of which surface here.
- [crates/policy-engine/src/loader.rs](../../crates/policy-engine/src/loader.rs) — 3 new tests. `static_loader_load_returns_new_yaml_bytes_after_set_yaml` walks three distinct YAML payloads through set_yaml + load and asserts each post-set load surfaces the new `id:` substring, the new content `drive.files.get`, AND that the prior id substrings are GONE (no append-only buffer regression). `file_loader_mtime_token_format_is_mtime_colon_nanos_for_grep` writes a tempfile, asserts `mtime:` prefix + all-digit non-empty nanos suffix, and pins that `version_token_sync` produces a byte-identical token to the async `load().version` (defense in depth across the two helpers). `policy_load_error_io_display_passes_inner_error_message_through_unchanged` pins both the Io variant (with `"permission denied (os error 13)"`-shape inner string) and the symmetric Backend variant (`"pg pool exhausted"`) — both must `starts_with` their prefix AND `ends_with` the inner string byte-for-byte.

**2026-05-18 (round 81) — 6 more pure-helper tests on policy-engine ops + trace trait-derive pins.** Two policy-engine modules where prior rounds (ops via the OpsAtom/OpsExpression baseline + rounds 7/52 indirect; trace via round-2's policy_trace baseline) had pinned the typical happy path but specific Default-construction, zero-input-template, and Copy/Eq trait-derive contracts the engine's hot path depends on remained unpinned. On `ops`: `OpsAtom` derives both Serialize and Deserialize but the existing tests only exercise the parse/to_canonical pair — a refactor that camelCased the field names via `#[serde(rename_all = "camelCase")]` would silently break the upstream `provenance-plane` CBOR consumer that JSON-decodes via the literal field names, and the existing tests would still pass; `OpsExpression::default()` produces an empty `required` Vec, but no test directly asserted that the Default value is *trivially satisfied by ANY leaf set* (including empty) — the property every Layer-A no-required-ops policy depends on, where a refactor adding a sentinel "deny" atom to the Default value would mass-deny every block-only policy on next reload; `OpsExpression::resolve(&[], &ctx)` with zero templates is the load-bearing shape for Layer-B-only policies (the most common policy author shape — a `block` rule that doesn't care about chain authority), and a "must declare at least one required_op" guard regression would silently reject every block-only policy. On `trace`: `PolicyEvalMode::Comprehensive` was never directly compared against `FailFast` (the existing test pinned only the Default value) — a refactor that collapsed the variants would silently break the dashboard's "explain this denial" replay; `PolicyEvalMode` and `PolicyLayer` both derive `Copy`, which is load-bearing for the `set_layer` helper's `iter_mut().find(|l| l.layer == layer)` closure in [adapters/policy_trace.rs](../../crates/proxy/src/adapters/policy_trace.rs) — a refactor that dropped `Copy` would surface as borrow-check errors at hundreds of engine call sites rather than at this single trait-bound assertion, and the three-variant distinct-equality walk catches an alias regression (LayerA/LayerB merge mistake). Files:

- [crates/policy-engine/src/ops.rs](../../crates/policy-engine/src/ops.rs) — 3 new tests. `ops_atom_serde_json_round_trip_carries_three_named_fields_no_extras` pins the wire shape directly via `serde_json::to_string` round-trip + an `as_object().unwrap().len() == 3` exact-field-count assertion (catches a derive that added an extra field via `#[serde(skip_serializing_if = ...)]` regression). `ops_expression_default_is_empty_required_vec_trivially_satisfied` walks `is_satisfied_by` against three leaf-set shapes: empty (no leaf ops), single-leaf, and multi-leaf with distinct schemes — all must pass against the Default. `ops_expression_resolve_with_empty_templates_list_returns_empty_required` pins both the construction path (zero templates → empty required Vec) AND the composed contract (the resulting expression is trivially satisfied — composes with the Default test to cover both construction paths).
- [crates/policy-engine/src/trace.rs](../../crates/policy-engine/src/trace.rs) — 3 new tests. `policy_eval_mode_comprehensive_variant_compares_distinct_from_fail_fast` walks both reflexive equalities (FailFast == FailFast, Comprehensive == Comprehensive) AND the cross-variant inequality. `policy_eval_mode_is_copy_at_use_sites` pins via the canonical `fn require_copy<T: Copy>()` trait-bound check (zero-cost — no instantiation) plus a use-by-value sanity (`let a = ...; let b = a; assert_eq!(a, b)` would fail-compile on move). `policy_layer_is_copy_at_use_sites_and_distinct_across_three_variants` pins the symmetric Copy bound on `PolicyLayer` AND the three-variant pairwise-distinct equality walk (LayerA != LayerB != ReadFilter, plus reflexive for each).

proxy bin tests: **706 → 706** (no changes in proxy crate). policy-engine lib tests: **149 → 155**. Gate floors unchanged (workspace 45/55, per-crate proxy 45 / shared-types 95 / policy-engine 88 / cli 10). `cargo fmt --all --check`, `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets --locked -- -D warnings -A clippy::type_complexity -A clippy::too_many_arguments -A clippy::result_large_err`, `cargo test --workspace --locked`, and `RUSTFLAGS="-D warnings" cargo build --workspace --release --locked` all green.

**2026-05-18 (round 82) — 6 more pure-helper tests on auth_middleware AuthFail Display-prefix pins + api/blocked ApiError Display-prefix pins.** Two modules where prior rounds (16+52+76 for auth_middleware; 5+6+60+71 for api/blocked) had pinned the Display *inner-string* passthrough (e.g. `assert!(s.contains("network timeout"))`) but the *prefix half* of every `#[error("<prefix>: {0}")]` annotation — the operator-facing log-filter substring — remained unpinned across both error enums. On `auth_middleware::AuthFail`: the three `<prefix>: {0}` variants (`Refresh` → `"google token refresh failed: "`, `CatKey` → `"CAT key fetch failed: "`, `Other` → `"internal: "`) are what Grafana / Loki filters key on to bucket bearer-refresh failures separately from CAT-key fetch failures separately from catch-all internal errors — a refactor that softened any prefix (the natural "tidy up error messages" mistake) would silently collapse three distinct dashboards onto one. On `api/blocked::ApiError`: the `NotFound` variant's exact `"not found"` Display string (no variant-name prefix, no trailing punctuation — operator log filters key on the exact substring as the "row already resolved or expired" signal); the `PicRefused` variant's `"PIC invariant refused: "` prefix (spec.md §6.6 escape-hatch surface; log aggregators bucket "operator override refused by Trust Plane" on this prefix separately from policy decisions which never use the "invariant" qualifier); the `Internal` variant's `"internal: "` prefix (catch-all for "this shouldn't happen" code paths — must NOT collapse to `Db`'s transparent shape, since Grafana distinguishes "proxy bug" from "postgres health issue" on prefix presence). Files:

- [crates/proxy/src/auth_middleware.rs](../../crates/proxy/src/auth_middleware.rs) — 3 new tests. `auth_fail_refresh_display_carries_google_token_refresh_failed_prefix` pins the full `"google token refresh failed: network timeout"` Display via `assert_eq!`, not just `contains` (the existing test only pinned inner substring). `auth_fail_cat_key_display_carries_cat_key_fetch_failed_prefix` walks three distinct inner messages (`"trust plane 503"`, `"expected 32 bytes"`, `"transport: connection refused"`) so a refactor that hardcoded the prefix into a smaller uniform wrapper surfaces on at least one of the three inputs. `auth_fail_other_display_carries_internal_prefix_with_inner_message` pins the catch-all `"internal: {0}"` shape — the only variant prefix that distinguishes "proxy bug, file an issue" from the more-specific external-system variants.
- [crates/proxy/src/api/blocked.rs](../../crates/proxy/src/api/blocked.rs) — 3 new tests. `api_error_not_found_display_is_exact_not_found_string` pins the exact two-word `"not found"` Display (no variant-name prefix) — distinct from the wire-shape `error` field which IS `"blocked action not found"` (the operator-UI title); the Display is the log substring, they're intentionally distinct. `api_error_pic_refused_display_carries_pic_invariant_refused_prefix` pins the full `"PIC invariant refused: ops not subset of predecessor: missing [drive:write:bob/*]"` shape via `assert_eq!` against the canonical Trust-Plane-emitted missing-ops syntax. `api_error_internal_display_carries_internal_prefix_with_inner_string` pins the `"internal: unexpected None on session lookup"` shape — the prefix is what keeps Internal from collapsing to Db's transparent shape on operator dashboards.
