Gate floors unchanged (workspace 45/55, per-crate proxy 45 / shared-types 95 / policy-engine 88 / cli 10). `cargo fmt --all --check`, `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets --locked -- -D warnings -A clippy::type_complexity -A clippy::too_many_arguments -A clippy::result_large_err`, `cargo test --workspace --locked`, and `RUSTFLAGS="-D warnings" cargo build --workspace --release --locked` all green.

**2026-05-18 (round 83) — 6 more pure-helper tests on adapters/error AppError Display-prefix pins (the third and final error enum after rounds 82's ApiError + AuthFail).** [crates/proxy/src/adapters/error.rs](../../crates/proxy/src/adapters/error.rs) had 14 tests covering `.code()` + `.status()` + `.body()` for every variant + `into_response()` + `upstream_error_kind`, but ZERO of them pinned the `Display` half — every `#[error("...")]` annotation was untested. Operator log filters key on the Display *prefix* (Grafana / Loki splits buckets on substrings like `"Trust Plane refused PCA:"` vs `"policy engine:"` vs `"policy ops template:"`); a refactor that "harmonized" prefixes for consistency would silently merge dashboards onto each other. Six variant prefixes pinned: `PolicyBlocked` (uses `{policy_id:?}` Debug-form which renders Option as `Some("p1")` / `None` — operators grep both shapes; a switch to `{policy_id}` would either panic or require unwrap_or which silently merges the two buckets), `RequireConfirmation` (`"requires user confirmation: "`), `PicInvariantViolation` (`"Trust Plane refused PCA: "` — distinct from `OAuthError::PicInvariant`'s prefix so adapter-side monotonicity faults don't merge with OAuth-side ones on dashboards), `Policy` (`"policy engine: "` for engine-evaluation errors), `OpsTemplate` (`"policy ops template: "` for operator-authored template faults — distinct from `Policy` to let runbooks distinguish "your YAML is malformed" from "the engine rejected your structure" without re-walking the trace), `Internal` (`"internal error: <inner>"` — explicitly INCLUDES the inner String, asymmetric to `OAuthError::Internal` which masks it; the asymmetry is intentional since AppError surfaces to authenticated agents and the structured 500 response collapses to a no-detail title via `body()`). Files:

- [crates/proxy/src/adapters/error.rs](../../crates/proxy/src/adapters/error.rs) — 6 new tests. `app_error_policy_blocked_display_carries_policy_id_debug_form_and_reason` walks both `Some(_)` and `None` policy_id shapes and pins exact `Some("p1")` / `None` Debug-form rendering. `app_error_require_confirmation_display_carries_prefix_with_inner_reason` pins the full Display via `assert_eq!`. `app_error_pic_invariant_violation_display_carries_trust_plane_prefix` pins against the canonical missing-atoms syntax operators see in upstream Trust Plane refusal bodies. `app_error_policy_display_carries_policy_engine_prefix_with_inner_error_message` constructs a real `rego::Error::BadDecision`, pins the `"policy engine: "` prefix AND the inner `rego::Error` Display passthrough (`"invalid decision shape:"`) so cross-module drift surfaces. `app_error_ops_template_display_carries_policy_ops_template_prefix` constructs a real `OpsParseError::UnknownVar`, pins the symmetric inner-Display passthrough on the sibling arm. `app_error_internal_display_carries_internal_error_prefix_with_inner_string` pins the full `"internal error: <inner>"` shape and explicitly documents the asymmetry with `OAuthError::Internal`.

proxy bin tests: **712 → 718**. policy-engine lib tests unchanged. Cumulative across rounds 1–83: proxy **+561**, cli +17, policy-engine +97, shared-types +13 — **688 new pure-helper unit tests** total.

**2026-05-18 (round 84) — 6 more pure-helper tests on pic/verifier VerifierError Display-prefix + named-field pins (the chain-walker error enum, completing the round-79/82/83 Display sweep across error.rs / api_error / oauth_error / app_error / auth_fail / api_blocked / app_error and now verifier).** [crates/proxy/src/pic/verifier.rs](../../crates/proxy/src/pic/verifier.rs) already had `verifier_error_display_carries_named_field_values` pinning two of the eight variants' inner-field substring rendering, but six of the eight `#[error("...")]` annotations — every prefix half plus the full single-line Display shape of the multi-line `ContinuityBroken` attribute — were untested. Operator chain-walker dashboards split buckets on these exact substrings: `"pca <uuid> not found"` vs `"CAT signature failed to verify on pca <uuid>"` vs `"continuity broken between pca <child> and predecessor <parent>: provenance.cat_sig mismatch"` vs `"p_0 changed across hop (<child> != <parent>)"` vs `"decoding signed PCA bytes: <inner>"` vs `"CAT key fetch: <inner>"`. A refactor that "harmonized" any prefix (the natural "tidy up error messages" mistake — collapsing `"CAT signature failed"` and `"CAT key fetch"` into a shared `"CAT: ..."` shape, or softening `"continuity broken"` to `"chain break"`) would silently merge dashboards onto each other and force operators to cross-reference the structured trace just to recover what variant fired. Six variants pinned with exact `assert_eq!` shapes: `Missing(Uuid)` (uuid renders via lowercase-hyphenated Display, not Debug-braced form — a `{0:?}` regression would silently wrap it in `Uuid("...")` and break log filters keyed on the bare uuid substring); `BadCatSignature(Uuid)` (symmetric to Missing on the uuid-rendering contract but distinct prefix); `ContinuityBroken { child, parent }` (the only multi-line `thiserror` attribute in the enum — pin that Display collapses to a single line with both uuids AND the `": provenance.cat_sig mismatch"` suffix preserved verbatim); `P0Mismatch { child_p0, parent_p0 }` (parenthesized inequality with child-first / parent-second ordering matching `HopOrder`'s parameter order for operator muscle memory); `Decode(String)` (`"decoding signed PCA bytes: "` prefix + inner CBOR-decoder triage message passthrough); `CatKey(String)` (`"CAT key fetch: "` prefix — distinct from `BadCatSignature`'s `"CAT signature failed"` so operators bucket "key unavailable" separately from "key disagreed"). Files:

- [crates/proxy/src/pic/verifier.rs](../../crates/proxy/src/pic/verifier.rs) — 6 new tests. `verifier_error_missing_display_carries_pca_not_found_prefix_and_uuid`, `verifier_error_bad_cat_signature_display_carries_pca_uuid_and_signature_prefix`, `verifier_error_continuity_broken_display_renders_both_named_uuids_and_full_suffix`, `verifier_error_p0_mismatch_display_carries_full_shape_with_both_principals`, `verifier_error_decode_display_carries_decoding_signed_pca_prefix_with_inner_string`, `verifier_error_cat_key_display_carries_cat_key_fetch_prefix_with_inner_string` — each pins the full Display via `assert_eq!` against a known constructor input so any prefix-softening or named-field-ordering refactor surfaces immediately.

proxy bin tests: **718 → 724**. policy-engine lib tests unchanged. Cumulative across rounds 1–84: proxy **+567**, cli +17, policy-engine +97, shared-types +13 — **694 new pure-helper unit tests** total. Gate floors unchanged (workspace 45/55, per-crate proxy 45 / shared-types 95 / policy-engine 88 / cli 10). `cargo fmt --all --check`, `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets --locked -- -D warnings -A clippy::type_complexity -A clippy::too_many_arguments -A clippy::result_large_err`, `cargo test --workspace --locked`, and `RUSTFLAGS="-D warnings" cargo build --workspace --release --locked` all green.
**2026-05-18 (round 85) — 6 more pure-helper tests on policy-engine match_expr MatchError Display-prefix + named-field + Error::source chain pins (the match-expression evaluator's error enum, sibling to round-84's verifier sweep).** [crates/policy-engine/src/match_expr.rs](../../crates/policy-engine/src/match_expr.rs) had 30+ tests pinning `evaluate()` happy paths plus extensive `matches!(err, MatchError::BadShape { ref op, .. } if op == ...)` shape assertions, but ZERO directly pinned the `#[error("...")]` Display output for any of the three variants, AND the `Error::source()` chain that `Template(#[from] OpsParseError)` is supposed to wire was never directly asserted. The Display strings are what the dashboard's "policy authoring errors" panel renders verbatim to the operator (the engine itself never surfaces these on the public wire — they flow up through `AppError::Policy` / `AppError::OpsTemplate` to per-policy debug tooling), and the `source()` chain is what makes anyhow-style walkers in adapter code able to recover the inner `OpsParseError` without parsing the Display string. Six surfaces pinned: `UnsupportedOp(String)` — the BACKTICKED operator name (` `{0}` ` syntax) is load-bearing for operator log filters to distinguish "operator typo" from `BadShape`-style faults, and a refactor that dropped the backticks "for consistency with the other variants" would silently merge two distinct Loki buckets; `BadShape { op, expected, got }` named-field rendering order (op/expected/got matches the struct field declaration order — pin all three values distinct so a `expected/got/op` flip "for grammar" surfaces); `BadShape.expected: &'static str` type contract across four typical descriptors (a refactor to `String` would surface as compile errors at construction sites — `&'static str` keeps the variant cheap-Clone for retry tracing); `Template(#[from] OpsParseError)` distinct prefix `"template error: "` vs the sibling adapter-layer `AppError::OpsTemplate` prefix `"policy ops template: "` (the two prefixes are intentionally different layers — engine-side inline match-expr template faults vs adapter-side required_ops template faults — and a "harmonize" refactor would silently merge the two buckets); `BadShape.op: String` accepts dotted field paths (`"user.contact.email"`) — a refactor that canonicalized to the last segment for "display brevity" would silently drop the path context operators rely on to locate the offending clause; `std::error::Error::source()` chain — `Template` returns Some via `#[from]`, leaf variants `UnsupportedOp`/`BadShape` return None (a hand-rolled `From` impl that omitted `source()` wiring would surface here). Files:

- [crates/policy-engine/src/match_expr.rs](../../crates/policy-engine/src/match_expr.rs) — 6 new tests. `match_error_unsupported_op_display_carries_backticked_operator`, `match_error_bad_shape_display_renders_all_three_named_fields_in_order`, `match_error_bad_shape_display_static_str_expected_field_supports_typical_descriptors` (walks four descriptors and pins prefix-suffix bracketing), `match_error_template_display_carries_template_error_prefix_with_inner_passthrough` (cross-module prefix-distinction pin against `AppError::OpsTemplate`), `match_error_bad_shape_display_op_field_supports_dotted_field_paths`, `match_error_implements_std_error_trait_for_source_chain_walking` (asserts `source()` returns None for leaf arms and Some for the `Template` arm with the inner Display passing through).

**2026-05-18 (round 86) — 6 more pure-helper tests on policy-engine rego::Error Display-prefix + named-field + transparent-arm passthrough pins (the engine entrypoint's error enum, sibling to round-79/85's decision/match_expr sweep).** [crates/policy-engine/src/rego.rs](../../crates/policy-engine/src/rego.rs) had 25+ tests pinning `Engine::evaluate()` happy paths plus `matches!(err, Error::BadDecision(_))` / `Error::BadRegex { .. }` shape assertions, but ZERO directly pinned the `#[error("...")]` Display output for the six variants. The Display strings flow through `AppError::Policy`'s `"policy engine: "` adapter-side wrapper (round 83 pinned that), so the composed log line operators read is `"policy engine: invalid decision shape: <inner>"` (two-prefix stack giving a runbook the engine-vs-decision-shape axis split). Round 85 pinned the sibling `match_expr::MatchError` Display surfaces; this round completes the two-module sweep across the engine's primary error types. Six surfaces pinned: `Yaml(#[from] serde_yaml::Error)` — `"YAML parse error: "` prefix + inner serde_yaml position-info passthrough (constructed against a real failing serde_yaml parse and checked via suffix-after-prefix byte-identity to the inner Display, so operator tools that split on `": "` to recover line+column info don't silently break); `BadDecision(String)` — `"invalid decision shape: "` prefix (distinct from a future generic `"bad decision"` softening — runbook substring split on `"invalid decision shape"` keys on the structure-validation specificity); `BadReadFilter(String)` — `"invalid read_filter: "` prefix with the UNDERSCORE-form `read_filter` matching the YAML key authors wrote (a refactor to space-form `"read filter"` for "human-readability" would silently break the operator's cross-reference into the YAML); `BadRegex { pat, source }` — BACKTICKED `{pat}` field is load-bearing for delimiting pattern boundaries when the pattern itself contains a colon (the field separator), AND the `{source}` substitution carries the regex compiler's actionable triage ("unbalanced parenthesis" / "missing character class"); `Match(#[from] MatchError)` — `#[error(transparent)]` forwards Display BYTE-IDENTICALLY to the inner (cross-module drift surfaces via the explicit `assert_eq!` against round-85's pinned `"unsupported operator `weird_op`"` shape); `Ops(#[from] OpsParseError)` — symmetric `transparent` arm where `Error::source()` is forwarded to the inner leaf (returns None since `OpsParseError::UnknownVar` is a leaf — pin that contract so a refactor that swapped `transparent` for an explicit prefix wrapper would silently start exposing the leaf via `source()` and break anyhow-style walkers in adapter code that expect a fresh `source()` link only at the prefixed arms). Files:

- [crates/policy-engine/src/rego.rs](../../crates/policy-engine/src/rego.rs) — 6 new tests. `rego_error_yaml_display_carries_yaml_parse_error_prefix_with_inner_serde_message` (byte-identity suffix check against the inner serde_yaml Display), `rego_error_bad_decision_display_carries_invalid_decision_shape_prefix`, `rego_error_bad_read_filter_display_carries_invalid_read_filter_prefix`, `rego_error_bad_regex_display_renders_pat_in_backticks_and_full_source_message` (constructs a real `regex::Regex::new("(unbalanced")` failure and pins both the backticked-pat prefix AND the inner regex::Error Display passthrough), `rego_error_match_arm_transparent_display_strips_no_prefix` (cross-module drift surface against round 85), `rego_error_ops_arm_transparent_display_forwards_inner_and_source_chain` (pins `transparent` Display passthrough AND the `source()` is None contract for the leaf-wrapping arm).

Gate floors unchanged (workspace 45/55, per-crate proxy 45 / shared-types 95 / policy-engine 88 / cli 10). `cargo fmt --all --check`, `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets --locked -- -D warnings -A clippy::type_complexity -A clippy::too_many_arguments -A clippy::result_large_err`, `cargo test --workspace --locked`, and `RUSTFLAGS="-D warnings" cargo build --workspace --release --locked` all green.

**2026-05-18 (round 87) — 6 more pure-helper tests on adapters/policy_trace mutation primitives + summary fallback + asymmetric helper contracts.** [crates/proxy/src/adapters/policy_trace.rs](../../crates/proxy/src/adapters/policy_trace.rs) already had 10 tests covering the happy paths (replace-passed-entry, append-when-absent, the per-helper `passed`/`failed` arm choice, the comma-join in `summary`), but six operator-actionable surfaces remained unpinned: the `.unwrap_or("unknown")` fallback in `summary()` when a `LayerOutcome` carries `passed: false` AND `error_code: None` (the "trace was hand-mutated without a code" tripwire — operators bucket on `=unknown` to distinguish from any future named code, and a refactor that collapsed the fallback to an empty string would silently produce `layer_b=` and break log-derived metrics); the `mark_layer_a_failed` helper's hardcoded `None` for `matched_rule_id` (the dashboard's "PCA refused" alert keys on `layer_a` rows with null rule id as the discriminant against any future Layer A variant that carries a policy id, and a refactor that "for consistency with mark_read_filter" added a policy_id parameter would silently start populating the field); the `mark_read_filter(blocked=false)` arm's four-axis shape (`passed=true` + `error_code=None` + `matched_rule_id=Some` + `detail=Some` — the ONLY place in the adapter where a passed layer surfaces a detail, since the public `LayerOutcome::passed()` constructor drops detail; a refactor that routed through `LayerOutcome::passed()` for "consistency" would silently strip the per-event quarantine count from the structured log); the `set_layer` single-replace semantic when duplicates exist (`iter_mut().find(...)` returns first match; a refactor to `filter().for_each()` would replace-all and mask malformed traces — pin the current single-replace as the correct contract); the single-element `summary` join with no trailing separator (the empty-Vec case was pinned by `summary_renders_read_filter_label_and_empty_layers`; this fills in the single-element boundary against a refactor that built the string with a manual `for { push(',') ... }` loop); the symmetric `mark_read_filter(blocked=true, matched_policy_id=None)` arm — the dashboard distinguishes per-policy hits from global-scanner hits on the presence of `matched_rule_id`, and a refactor that unwrap_or-defaulted to `"unknown"` would silently merge the buckets. Files:

- [crates/proxy/src/adapters/policy_trace.rs](../../crates/proxy/src/adapters/policy_trace.rs) — 6 new tests. `summary_renders_unknown_for_failed_layer_with_no_error_code` (constructs a hand-rolled LayerOutcome with passed=false + error_code=None and pins the `=unknown` fallback), `mark_layer_a_failed_always_sets_matched_rule_id_to_none` (pins the hardcoded-None contract), `mark_read_filter_passed_arm_carries_none_error_code_with_detail_present` (pins the four-axis passed-with-detail shape), `set_layer_replaces_only_first_matching_entry_when_duplicates_exist` (pins single-replace semantic against a deliberate-duplicate trace), `summary_single_failed_layer_renders_without_trailing_separator` (single-element join boundary), `mark_read_filter_blocked_with_none_policy_id_omits_matched_rule_id` (symmetric None arm).

**2026-05-18 (round 88) — 6 more pure-helper tests on oauth/error OAuthError Display tightening + previously-uncovered variant Display pins (the OAuth handler's error enum's remaining Display gaps after round 79's body-boundary sweep).** [crates/proxy/src/oauth/error.rs](../../crates/proxy/src/oauth/error.rs) had 13 tests covering `status()`, `body().code`, `body().detail`, the BadRequest/Internal/Upstream body boundaries (round 79), and an existing `oauth_error_display_strings_pinned_for_log_filters` test that pinned five variants via `.contains(...)` substring checks — but four variants had NO Display assertion at all (`BadRequest`, `BridgeRejected`, `Db`, `Crypto`, `Upstream`'s Display string) and the five substring-pinned variants were vulnerable to a refactor that prepended a wrapper prefix or trailing punctuation (which `.contains()` would silently allow). Operator log filters key on these prefixes to bucket OAuth-handler faults across multiple Grafana panels; tightening the assertions to `assert_eq!` against the full Display shape catches the "softened to consistent prefix" refactor class. Six surfaces pinned: `BadRequest(String)` — full `"invalid request: {0}"` shape (the title in `body()` is `"invalid request"` but the wire `code` is `"bad_request"`; pin the Display matches the title-form, NOT the code-form, so a "consistency with code" refactor surfaces); `BridgeRejected(String)` — `"federation token rejected: {0}"` prefix had ZERO test coverage (operator dashboards bucket federation-bridge JWT validation failures on the `"federation token"` qualifier separately from upstream-call and PIC monotonicity failures); `Db(#[from] sqlx::Error)` — `"database error"` no-leak contract (symmetric to round 79's `Internal` mask: the inner sqlx::Error Display carries schema/query/constraint internals — pin the wrapper Display does NOT contain the sqlx "no rows" substring); `Crypto` unit variant — fixed `"crypto failure"` two-word Display (no detail; pin so a refactor that promoted to `Crypto(String)` for "consistency with the detail-carrying arms" surfaces); `Upstream(#[from] reqwest::Error)` — `"upstream call failed"` URL-no-leak contract (symmetric to round 79's body-boundary pin; constructed against a real reqwest::Error with a sentinel `secret-path` substring and `hunter2` credential, pin both are absent from the wrapper Display); five substring-pinned variants (`PkceFail`/`SessionGone`/`UnknownClient`/`BadAuthCode`/`PicInvariant`) tightened to full `assert_eq!` shapes so a refactor that prepended `"oauth: "` or appended `"."` would surface (the existing `.contains()` checks would silently allow either). Files:

- [crates/proxy/src/oauth/error.rs](../../crates/proxy/src/oauth/error.rs) — 6 new tests. `oauth_error_bad_request_display_carries_invalid_request_prefix_with_inner_string`, `oauth_error_bridge_rejected_display_carries_federation_token_rejected_prefix`, `oauth_error_db_display_does_not_carry_inner_sqlx_string` (no-leak symmetric to Internal), `oauth_error_crypto_display_is_fixed_no_detail_form`, `oauth_error_upstream_display_masks_inner_reqwest_url_for_no_secret_leak` (URL no-leak via real reqwest::Error with sentinel `secret-path`/`hunter2`/`192.0.2.1` substrings asserted absent), `oauth_error_display_tightened_for_five_substring_pinned_variants` (PkceFail/SessionGone/UnknownClient/BadAuthCode all unit-shaped via `assert_eq!`; PicInvariant via the full prefix+inner shape).

Gate floors unchanged (workspace 45/55, per-crate proxy 45 / shared-types 95 / policy-engine 88 / cli 10). `cargo fmt --all --check`, `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets --locked -- -D warnings -A clippy::type_complexity -A clippy::too_many_arguments -A clippy::result_large_err`, `cargo test --workspace --locked`, and `RUSTFLAGS="-D warnings" cargo build --workspace --release --locked` all green.

**2026-05-18 (round 89) — 6 more pure-helper tests on policy-engine yaml schema PolicyDoc Clone + override-rename + empty-array + default_pic_mode + empty-list Some-vs-None + closed-enum reject.** [crates/policy-engine/src/yaml.rs](../../crates/policy-engine/src/yaml.rs) already had 17 tests covering each individual field's parse path, default-value helper symmetry (Mode + QuarantineActionCfg), the kebab/snake wire-string distinction, and the burst-cfg empty-object boundary — but six operator-actionable surfaces remained unpinned: the PolicyDoc Clone derive across every optional field together (the dashboard's policy-list endpoint clones for the wire-serialize, and a refactor that switched any field to Cow or dropped the derive would surface as a borrow-checker rewrite at call sites rather than at a single trait-bound assertion); the `#[serde(rename = "override")]` attribute on `override_` (Rust's reserved-keyword escape — pin BOTH directions so a refactor that dropped the rename would silently start emitting `override_` on serialize and rejecting `override` on deserialize, fail-parsing every existing operator-authored policy); the empty top-level YAML array (`[]`) is a VALID input that returns an empty Vec (the empty-engine boot path `Engine::new("[]")` depends on this — a refactor that required at least one policy would silently break every test fixture and dev-default); the `default_pic_mode` helper's direct return value (symmetric to the existing `default_quarantine_action_helper_returns_replace_with_marker` test — the existing `defaults_match_safe_production_posture` test pins it but isn't dedicated, and the Audit default is the safe-posture contract a refactor flipping to RuntimeGate would silently break for every customer); the `to: []` explicit-empty-list distinction from None on `RecipientsCfg.to` (the notifier logic distinguishes "operator explicitly set empty — suppress all email to this list" from "inherit from global" — a refactor that collapsed empty to None for "treat empty as unset" would silently start email-blasting an operator who deliberately blanked a list); the `AuditBodyMode` closed-enum reject of unknown variants (symmetric to the existing `parse_policies_rejects_unknown_mode_value` on Mode — pin that `audit_body: hashing` typos and kebab/snake-mismatch values fail-parse rather than silently forward-compat into Hash, since silent compat would leave the privacy default in force without operator knowledge). Files:

- [crates/policy-engine/src/yaml.rs](../../crates/policy-engine/src/yaml.rs) — 6 new tests. `policy_doc_clone_preserves_all_optional_fields_independently` (round-trips a full-shape policy through Clone and verifies every optional field plus the inner serde_yaml::Value slots), `policy_doc_override_field_renamed_from_override_serde_attr_round_trip` (pins deserialize accepts `override` AND serialize emits `override` NOT `override_`), `parse_policies_empty_yaml_array_returns_zero_policies_without_error` (covers both `[]` and `--- []\n` renderings), `default_pic_mode_helper_returns_audit_directly` (symmetric to default_quarantine_action's dedicated pin), `recipients_cfg_empty_to_list_round_trips_as_some_empty_vec_distinct_from_none` (pins Some(empty) NOT None for explicit `to: []`, with cc/bcc covering the sibling shapes), `audit_body_unknown_variant_value_is_rejected_by_closed_enum` (pins both typo-extra-suffix and kebab/snake-mismatch failures).

proxy bin tests unchanged at 736. policy-engine lib tests: **167 → 173**. Cumulative across rounds 1–89: proxy +579, cli +17, policy-engine **+115**, shared-types +13 — **724 new pure-helper unit tests** total. Gate floors unchanged (workspace 45/55, per-crate proxy 45 / shared-types 95 / policy-engine 88 / cli 10). `cargo fmt --all --check`, `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets --locked -- -D warnings -A clippy::type_complexity -A clippy::too_many_arguments -A clippy::result_large_err`, `cargo test --workspace --locked`, and `RUSTFLAGS="-D warnings" cargo build --workspace --release --locked` all green.

**2026-05-18 (round 90) — 6 more pure-helper tests on oauth/bridge validate_federation_token payload-only contracts + FederationClaims Debug + state field round-trip.** [crates/proxy/src/oauth/bridge.rs](../../crates/proxy/src/oauth/bridge.rs) already had 15 tests covering the happy path + the three operator-visible failure modes (malformed-jwt-segments / bad-base64 / bad-claims missing-field) + clock-skew boundaries on `iat` and `exp` + the `iss`/`pca_0_cbor_b64` Option round-trips + `infer_idp` classifier across known/secondary/mixed-case/fallback shapes, but six surfaces remained unpinned. The module's docstring commits explicitly to "payload-only" validation as the M0/M1 stub contract (signature step stubbed so the swap to JWKS-backed verify is one function) — pin that contract end-to-end so a refactor that started inspecting the header or signature segments would surface here rather than only at downstream call sites. On `validate_federation_token`: the value-validation deserialize fault path (invalid uuid in `pca_0_id` rejects with `"bad claims"` — distinct from the existing missing-field test; a refactor that swapped to `pca_0_id: String` with deferred runtime parse would silently start accepting garbage); the header-segment ignore contract (three distinct header shapes — random b64, empty string, raw garbage — all MUST accept because the function never inspects the header); the signature-segment ignore contract (symmetric — three distinct signature shapes all accept); the extra-segments contract (5-part JWT with 4 dots accepts because the `(parts.next(), parts.next(), parts.next())` destructure takes only the first 3 and leaves the rest unread — pin the current behavior so a future tightening that enforced exactly-3-segments is a deliberate change). On `FederationClaims`: the Debug derive carries `pca_0_id` (uuid hyphenated-lowercase form via Uuid's Debug==Display) + `p_0` principal + struct name — operator forensics on the OAuth callback path keys on these substrings; a manual Debug impl that hid either field "in the name of don't log PII" would silently break the join across request_id and bridge-decoded principal; the `state` field byte-identical round-trip across three operator-correlation-id shapes (uuid-style, mixed-case opaque, embedded-separators) — a refactor that normalized the value (lowercased / trimmed / URL-decoded) would silently break operator dashboards that join the proxy's request_id against the bridge's emitted state. Files:

- [crates/proxy/src/oauth/bridge.rs](../../crates/proxy/src/oauth/bridge.rs) — 6 new tests. `validate_federation_token_with_invalid_uuid_pca_0_id_carries_bad_claims_msg` (value-validation distinct from missing-field), `validate_federation_token_ignores_header_content_payload_only_validation` (3 header shapes, all accept), `validate_federation_token_ignores_signature_content_payload_only_validation` (3 signature shapes, all accept), `validate_federation_token_accepts_jwt_with_extra_segments_using_first_three` (5-part JWT documents current first-3 behavior), `federation_claims_debug_carries_pca_0_id_and_p_0_for_log_grep` (Debug rendering includes uuid + principal + struct name), `validate_federation_token_state_field_round_trips_verbatim_through_payload` (3 distinct state shapes, byte-identical).

proxy bin tests: **736 → 742**. policy-engine lib tests unchanged at 173. Gate floors unchanged (workspace 45/55, per-crate proxy 45 / shared-types 95 / policy-engine 88 / cli 10). `cargo fmt --all --check`, `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets --locked -- -D warnings -A clippy::type_complexity -A clippy::too_many_arguments -A clippy::result_large_err`, `cargo test --workspace --locked`, and `RUSTFLAGS="-D warnings" cargo build --workspace --release --locked` all green.

**2026-05-18 (round 91) — 6 more pure-helper tests on policy-engine trace LayerOutcome constructors + OpsAtomView Clone independence + PolicyLayer closed-enum reject + PolicyTrace::new trace_id uniqueness.** [crates/policy-engine/src/trace.rs](../../crates/policy-engine/src/trace.rs) already had 14 tests covering the happy-path constructor shapes (LayerB-only `passed`, LayerB+full-info `failed`), `allowed()` boundaries (all-pass / any-fail / empty-layers / Decision::Block override), the snake_case wire shape for `PolicyLayer`, `PolicyEvalMode` Default + Copy + variant-distinct, `OpsAtomView` `From<&OpsAtom>` field round-trip + serde wire shape, and the `PolicyTrace` Debug/JSON envelope — but six operator-actionable surfaces remained unpinned. The trace module is the dashboard's per-request panel + `X-Proxilion-Trace-Id` join key; its public constructors and wire shape are stable contracts the operator UI depends on. Six surfaces pinned: `LayerOutcome::passed` symmetric coverage across all three `PolicyLayer` variants (existing test pins LayerB only — a refactor that switched one arm to layer-specific defaults like `matched_rule_id: Some("read_filter".into())` "for consistency" would silently start emitting a non-empty field on the wire for that arm only; pin all three layers route through identical empty Optional fields); `LayerOutcome::failed` with both Optional args as None (the adapter's read-filter dispatcher constructs this shape when the filter scanner reports an engine-internal fault that isn't attributable to a specific pattern; existing test pins the full-info shape only — a refactor that promoted matched_rule_id to non-Option defaulting to `""` "for consistency" would surface here; the error_code remains Some by contract since `failed()` is the ONLY wire path that surfaces an ErrorCode); `PolicyTrace::new` stamps a fresh `Uuid::new_v4()` per call (the dashboard's per-request lookup + the response header both depend on every eval producing a distinct id; a refactor that swapped to a once-cell static or to a hash-of-layers "deterministic id" cleanup would silently collapse every eval onto the same id and break per-request lookup — pin distinctness across 5 back-to-back calls via HashSet); `OpsAtomView` Clone deep-copy across all three String fields (the trace's `required_ops` Vec is cloned by the dashboard's wire-serialize path AND the adapter's Trust-Plane request builder — a refactor to `Cow<'static, str>` or `Arc<str>` for "cheaper clone" would surface here as the mutation aliasing back to the original; existing `ops_atom_view_round_trips_fields` pins `From<&OpsAtom>` not Clone); `PolicyLayer` closed-enum deserialize rejects 4 unknown variant strings (`"layer_c"` / `"layer-a"` hyphen-form / `"LayerA"` PascalCase / `""` empty-string) — a future `#[serde(other)]` forward-compat would silently bucket operator typos into a single fallback variant, surface any such drift; `LayerOutcome` failed-shape serde Value round-trip pins every named field (matched_rule_id / error_code / detail) on BOTH directions across distinct non-default values so a rename to `matched_id` or `message` "for tidying" surfaces here. Files:

- [crates/policy-engine/src/trace.rs](../../crates/policy-engine/src/trace.rs) — 6 new tests. `layer_outcome_passed_constructor_emits_each_of_three_layer_variants_with_identical_zero_fields` (iterates LayerA/LayerB/ReadFilter and pins identical empty Optional fields), `layer_outcome_failed_with_none_matched_rule_id_and_none_detail_carries_error_code_only` (minimum-info shape symmetric to existing full-info pin), `policy_trace_new_assigns_fresh_unique_trace_id_per_call` (HashSet across 5 calls), `ops_atom_view_clone_independence_after_field_mutation` (mutates original after clone, asserts clone unchanged across all three fields), `policy_layer_deserialize_rejects_unknown_variant_strings_for_closed_enum_contract` (4 bogus strings, all fail-parse), `layer_outcome_serde_round_trip_with_failed_shape_preserves_all_named_fields` (full-value round-trip via serde_json::Value with distinct non-default values on every field).

**2026-05-18 (round 92) — 6 more pure-helper tests on proxy error_envelope ErrorBody builder override symmetry + skip-predicate boundary + &'static str lifetime + chain-order independence.** [crates/proxy/src/error_envelope.rs](../../crates/proxy/src/error_envelope.rs) already had 12 tests covering the constructor + per-builder field set + selective serialize (only-set / all-set arms) + `DOCS_BASE` constant + Debug grep + `into_response` status (default 500 / explicit) + the `with_extras` override-on-repeat + the array/string extras values, but six operator-actionable surfaces remained unpinned. The envelope is the unified wire shape across EVERY 4xx/5xx in the proxy (per the file's docstring: "an operator pasting our body into a search bar should get to a fix in seconds") — its builder semantics and skip-predicate boundaries are stable contracts the operator runbooks depend on. Six surfaces pinned: `with_fix` override-on-repeat symmetric to the existing `with_extras` pin (adapter call sites occasionally layer a default fix from the engine with a more specific one from the adapter — a refactor that prepended/appended for "preserve operator history" would silently double the rendered string); `with_docs` override-on-repeat (the docs URL is a single canonical operator-copy-paste path — a refactor that built a `docs/?next=...&prev=...` chain would silently change the wire shape from one URL to a query-encoded pair and break operator workflow); `with_detail` override-on-repeat (the existing `with_detail_accepts_string_and_str_via_into` test pins the `impl Into<String>` accept shape but NOT the last-write-wins contract — a refactor to `format!("{old}; {new}")` "to preserve operator history" would silently double the rendered detail on every two-layer adapter); `with_extras` boolean-false / zero / empty-string skip-predicate boundary (the existing extras-skip test pins ONLY the null-omit + object-present arms — pin that the predicate is byte-narrowly `Value::is_null` not `is_falsy`, walking three distinct JSON-falsy values (`false`/`0`/`""`) all serialize as PRESENT on the wire; a refactor that tightened the predicate to "skip any falsy value" would silently drop these alternate shapes from operator visibility); `ErrorBody::new` &'static str lifetime contract on `error` + `code` (pinned via a `fn require_static<T: 'static>()` zero-cost trait-bound; the &'static contract keeps adapter hot-path envelope construction zero-alloc — a refactor to bare `String` would silently land an allocation per error body); chain-order independence across all four builders (the existing `builders_compose_and_set_individual_fields` pins ONE chain order — pin that the symmetric reverse order produces a byte-identical wire shape via `serde_json::Value` equality so a refactor that introduced a hidden dependency between builders ("with_fix only honors fix if detail is already set, for consistency") would surface here on the wire-shape diff). Files:

- [crates/proxy/src/error_envelope.rs](../../crates/proxy/src/error_envelope.rs) — 6 new tests. `with_fix_overrides_prior_value_on_repeated_call`, `with_docs_overrides_prior_value_on_repeated_call`, `with_detail_overrides_prior_value_on_repeated_call` (three symmetric override pins), `with_extras_boolean_false_serializes_as_present_distinct_from_null_skip` (walks `false`/`0`/`""` and asserts all three land on the wire as present + non-null), `error_body_required_fields_accept_static_str_literals_for_zero_alloc_paths` (compile-time trait-bound check via `fn require_static<T: 'static>()`), `builders_chain_in_any_order_and_compose_independently` (forward and reverse 4-builder chains compared via `serde_json::Value` equality for byte-identical wire shape).

**2026-05-18 (round 93) — 6 more pure-helper tests on proxy forwarder/nats sanitize_token allow-list reject coverage + ConnectError std::error trait + pub-tuple-field round-trip.** [crates/proxy/src/forwarder/nats.rs](../../crates/proxy/src/forwarder/nats.rs) already had 10 tests covering the alphanum-passthrough, hyphen/underscore preserve, dot-hierarchy preserve, NATS-wildcard (`*` / `>`) reject, partial whitespace (` ` / `\t` / `\n` / `\r`) reject, unicode-replacement, empty-input boundary, and the `ConnectError` Display + Debug shapes — but six operator-actionable surfaces remained unpinned. The `sanitize_token` function is the load-bearing boundary between the adapter-emitted `vendor.action` tokens and the NATS subject wire (`actions.<vendor>.<action>`) — a subscriber depth contract that wildcard subscriptions (`actions.>` / `actions.google.>`) depend on. Six surfaces pinned: full ASCII control-char range (0x00..=0x1F) sweep replacing each with `_` (the existing whitespace test only pins `\t` / `\n` / `\r` / `' '` by example — a refactor that narrowed the closure's reject set to a hand-rolled `matches!(c, ' ' | '\t' | '\n')` would silently let DEL / VT / FF / NUL through to the wire and many NATS clients reject the whole publish); ASCII-punctuation reject sweep across 27 distinct characters (`"`/`'`/backtick/parens/brackets/braces/slashes/colons/angles/`?`/`@`/`#`/`$`/`%`/`^`/`&`/`!`/`|`/`~`/`=`/`+`) — a refactor that widened the allow-list to "any printable ASCII" (a tempting "be lenient on inputs" change) would silently start landing quote chars and shell metacharacters on the NATS subject wire; all ten decimal digits preserved independently (a refactor that hand-rolled the range as `'1'..='9'` and dropped zero, for some "leading-zero-rejection" cleanup, would silently corrupt versioned vendor labels like `drive_v0`); byte-length-equals-input invariant for ASCII-only inputs across six representative shapes (a refactor that started escaping rejected chars with a multi-byte sequence like percent-encoding `%2A` for `*` would silently inflate subject lengths and break the wildcard subscription depth contract); `ConnectError` `std::error::Error` trait via dyn-cast + `source()` returns None leaf-contract (the boot path bubbles through anyhow chains — a refactor to `ConnectError(#[source] reqwest::Error)` "for anyhow-style chain walking" would change the source-arm-vs-leaf contract here as a deliberate wire-shape change); `pub String` tuple-field round-trip preserves byte-identical value (operator setup-status dashboards split on the inner message to surface category-specific hints — a refactor that normalized the inner via `.trim()` "for tidiness" would silently drop leading/trailing whitespace operators rely on for log grep). Files:

- [crates/proxy/src/forwarder/nats.rs](../../crates/proxy/src/forwarder/nats.rs) — 6 new tests. `sanitize_token_replaces_ascii_control_chars_with_underscore` (sweeps 0x00..=0x1F), `sanitize_token_replaces_punctuation_with_underscore_per_allow_list` (sweeps 27 punctuation chars), `sanitize_token_preserves_all_ten_decimal_digits_independently` (each digit walked separately), `sanitize_token_byte_length_equals_input_for_ascii_only_inputs` (6 representative shapes), `connect_error_implements_std_error_trait_for_anyhow_chain_walking` (dyn-cast + None-source leaf pin), `connect_error_inner_field_is_pub_and_round_trips_through_construction` (preserves whitespace + unicode + empty across 3 distinct inner strings).

**2026-05-18 (round 94) — 6 more pure-helper tests on proxy pic/cat_key CatKeyError std::error trait + Status boundary + Decode empty-inner + InfoResp strict-required + Fetch Display passthrough.** [crates/proxy/src/pic/cat_key.rs](../../crates/proxy/src/pic/cat_key.rs) already had 9 tests covering the Status / Decode Display strings, the 4xx/5xx Status variant distinction, `InfoResp` happy-path deserialize + unknown-field forward-compat, `CatKeyRegistry::new` construction with trailing-slash URLs, the `Arc`-clone shared-OnceCell semantics, and the connect-refused `Fetch(_)` matcher — but six operator-actionable surfaces remained unpinned. The `CatKeyError` type is the OAuth callback path's structured-error wire boundary (PIC chain verify → operator audit logs); its trait surface and Display contracts are the join points between the bridge logs and the dashboard's incident view. Six surfaces pinned: `std::error::Error` trait via dyn-cast + `source()` returns None for `Status` and `Decode` leaf-arms (a refactor that swapped `thiserror::Error` for a hand-rolled `impl Display` would silently drop the trait impl, breaking `anyhow::Error` chain walking on every `?` operator in the callback path — surface here at the dyn-cast rather than at the far-away call site type mismatch); `Status(0)` and `Status(u16::MAX)` boundary Display (a refactor that formatted via `StatusCode::from_u16(code).unwrap()` would panic on `0` since `StatusCode` only accepts 100..1000 — turning an upstream-closure log line into a worker-thread crash; pin both the no-panic AND the verbatim-render contract); `Decode(String::new())` still renders the `CAT public key decode failed:` prefix (a refactor that gated rendering on a non-empty inner — `if self.0.is_empty() { return Ok(()) }` — would silently produce blank log lines that no operator filter could bucket); `InfoResp` strict-required reject of missing `kid` (no `#[serde(default)]` — a refactor that added one for "be permissive" would silently accept kid-less responses and the verifier would later fail-hard on the empty kid with a confusing log line far from the actual wire-shape mismatch); symmetric strict-required reject of missing `public_key` (a refactor that defaulted to empty would surface as a curve-point validation error downstream rather than as a wire-shape mismatch at decode time); `Fetch(#[from] reqwest::Error)` Display end-to-end through `?` carries the `Trust Plane info fetch failed: ` prefix AND a non-empty inner-reqwest reason (a refactor that hand-rolled the Display to mask the inner "for don't-leak-upstream-errors" reasoning would silently strip the actionable triage half from every operator log line). Files:

- [crates/proxy/src/pic/cat_key.rs](../../crates/proxy/src/pic/cat_key.rs) — 6 new tests. `cat_key_error_implements_std_error_trait_for_anyhow_chain_walking` (dyn-cast + None-source leaf pin across Status + Decode), `cat_key_error_status_zero_does_not_panic_and_renders_zero_for_grep` (0 + u16::MAX boundary sweep), `cat_key_error_decode_with_empty_inner_string_still_renders_prefix` (empty-inner prefix-survival pin), `info_resp_rejects_missing_required_kid_field` (strict-required wire-shape contract), `info_resp_rejects_missing_required_public_key_field` (symmetric strict-required), `cat_key_error_fetch_variant_display_carries_trust_plane_prefix_with_inner_reason` (`#[from]` Display passthrough end-to-end through a connect-refused localhost port).

**2026-05-18 (round 95) — 6 more pure-helper tests on policy-engine RequestContext Default + customer_domain head-exact short-circuit + lookup_list missing-key + body JSON-object fallback + full struct serde round-trip.** [crates/policy-engine/src/context.rs](../../crates/policy-engine/src/context.rs) already had 14 tests covering the `lookup` happy paths (customer_domain bare identifier, path.*, user.email, headers.*, body.* string-unquoting vs. JSON-repr fallback for scalar numbers/booleans), the `lookup_list` array/non-array/non-string-element branches + empty-array vs. None distinction, the `user.email == ""` sentinel contract, and the clone-preserves-customer_domain Trust-Plane invariant — but six operator-actionable surfaces remained unpinned. `RequestContext` is the load-bearing template-substitution surface every policy evaluation depends on; its Default constructor and head-matching contract are the join points between the embed-API test panel and production engine boot. Six surfaces pinned: `RequestContext::default()` yields all-empty-state across every field including the three HashMaps + nested UserCtx (the engine `Engine::new("[]")` boot path and the embed-API test fixtures both rely on default-construct — a refactor that pre-seeded a sentinel customer_domain like "example.com" "for tests" would silently bypass operator template-substitution checks and let a malformed policy pass review); `UserCtx::default()` symmetric pin (the embed-API builds the context piecewise — a refactor to `email: Option<String>` would break the `lookup("user.email") -> Some("")` sentinel contract pinned previously); `customer_domain` head-exact short-circuit (the bare-identifier branch lives BEFORE the `split_once('.')` — pin that `customer_domain.foo` and `customer_domain.` BOTH disengage the short-circuit and return None, so a refactor that switched to `starts_with("customer_domain")` "for prefix-matching consistency" would surface as silently matching every sub-field path); `lookup_list("body.absent")` returns None via the `self.body.get(tail)?` early-return, distinct from the existing non-array and non-string-element pins (a refactor that swapped to `unwrap_or(&Value::Null)` for "be permissive" would still return None via the `as_array()?` step but drop the missing-key log line operators rely on for policy template typo triage); `lookup` for a body field with a JSON-object value returns the JSON object literal via the `v.to_string()` fallback (existing pins only walk scalar number+boolean — the Object branch's brace/quote/key passthrough was unpinned); full `RequestContext` serde round-trip preserves every field including all three HashMap shapes + the nested UserCtx.groups Vec (the embed API serializes the context over JSON to the dashboard's "test policy" panel; a refactor that switched body's value type from `Value` to `String` would silently break the to_domains array shape on the wire). Files:

- [crates/policy-engine/src/context.rs](../../crates/policy-engine/src/context.rs) — 6 new tests. `request_context_default_yields_empty_state_for_engine_boot_path` (every field empty + lookup_list returns None), `user_ctx_default_yields_empty_email_and_no_groups` (symmetric pin), `lookup_customer_domain_subfield_returns_none_not_short_circuit` (head-exact match via `customer_domain.foo` + `customer_domain.` both None), `lookup_list_body_with_missing_key_returns_none_distinct_from_non_array` (distinct early-return path pinned), `lookup_body_with_json_object_value_returns_json_repr_not_unquoted` (Object branch of the `v.to_string()` fallback), `request_context_serde_round_trip_preserves_every_map_and_field` (full struct round-trip across path / body / headers / user / customer_domain).

**2026-05-18 (round 96) — 6 more pure-helper tests on policy-engine Decision PartialEq for RequireConfirmation + RateLimit + closed-enum kind reject + QuarantineAction/Pattern::Literal/ReadFilter Debug surfaces.** [crates/policy-engine/src/decision.rs](../../crates/policy-engine/src/decision.rs) already had 18 tests covering the Pattern is_match contracts (literal + regex, case-sensitivity, anchors, alternation, empty-haystack-empty-needle, regex Clone + Debug-source-for-grep), the Decision serde round-trip for every variant, the Block-variant PartialEq distinction axis (reason + override_allowed), the rate_limit deserialize-rejects-negative + u32-overflow contract, the QuarantineAction Copy/Eq trait pins, the ReadFilter Clone-pattern-independence pin, and the kind-wire-string stability snapshot — but six operator-actionable surfaces remained unpinned. The `Decision` enum is the policy engine's wire boundary to the adapter's response path; its PartialEq + closed-enum contract is what the dedup + idempotent-response cache rely on, and its Debug renders feed every operator log line that traces a per-request decision. Six surfaces pinned: `Decision::RequireConfirmation` PartialEq distinguishes variants by `reason` (existing pins only walk Block — a refactor that derived PartialEq ignoring reason "since the user-facing string drifts" would silently collapse "share external" + "share to anyone" prompts onto the same wire identity); `Decision::RateLimit` PartialEq distinguishes on BOTH `burst` AND `per_seconds` via four-corner shape (a refactor that hashed only burst "since per_seconds is always 60 in practice" would silently collapse two distinct rate budgets); closed-enum kind-tag reject of unknown values (`#[serde(tag = "kind", rename_all = "snake_case")]` has no `#[serde(other)]` — pin via three distinct unknown-tag shapes so a future `#[serde(other)]` forward-compat surfaces here rather than silently bucketing operator typos in the embed-API test panel); `QuarantineAction` Debug carries variant-name substring for all three arms (the adapter's `tracing::info!(?action, ..)` log line keys on the variant name — a manual Debug that rendered the integer tag for "tidiness" would silently collapse the three variants onto an opaque integer in every quarantine-event log); `Pattern::Literal` Debug surfaces the inner needle (symmetric to the existing `pattern_regex_debug_includes_regex_source_for_operator_grep` — the violation log line is the operator's only triage handle on WHICH literal fired; a manual Debug that hid the inner "may contain user PII" would silently strip that triage); `ReadFilter` Debug renders BOTH `quarantine_patterns` slot AND `quarantine_action` variant name (the boot-time policy-load `tracing::debug!(?cfg, ..)` log line — a custom Debug that surfaced only `quarantine_patterns.len()` "for brevity" would silently hide the compiled-regex source from operators). Files:

- [crates/policy-engine/src/decision.rs](../../crates/policy-engine/src/decision.rs) — 6 new tests. `decision_partial_eq_distinguishes_require_confirmation_variants_by_reason`, `decision_partial_eq_distinguishes_rate_limit_variants_by_burst_and_per_seconds` (four-corner shape across baseline / same / diff-burst / diff-per), `decision_deserialize_rejects_unknown_kind_value` (3 distinct unknown-tag shapes), `quarantine_action_debug_carries_variant_name_for_log_grep` (all three variants), `pattern_literal_debug_carries_needle_for_operator_grep`, `read_filter_debug_surfaces_both_patterns_slot_and_quarantine_action`.

**2026-05-18 (round 97) — 6 more pure-helper tests on policy-engine ops.rs is_satisfied_by required-order preservation + OpsExpression::resolve UnknownVar propagation + OpsAtom empty-middle reject + Clone independence on OpsAtom and OpsExpression + std::error trait.** [crates/policy-engine/src/ops.rs](../../crates/policy-engine/src/ops.rs) already had 18 tests covering the template resolve happy path + unknown-var failure + list-valued single-expansion + scalar-fall-through + two-list-vars reject + empty-list-yields-zero + OpsAtom parse-each-malformed-shape + extra-colons-and-slashes-in-object + to_canonical round-trip + OpsParseError Display strings + MissingOps Display + is_satisfied_by empty-required pass + collect_vars left-to-right order + substitute literal-passthrough + unclosed-var + OpsAtom serde three-named-fields + OpsExpression::default empty-required + OpsExpression::resolve empty-templates-list — but six operator-actionable surfaces remained unpinned. The ops module is the Layer A cross-check engine; its `is_satisfied_by` ordering + Clone independence on OpsAtom/OpsExpression + `?` propagation through `resolve` are the load-bearing surfaces the adapter's Trust-Plane request builder and the PIC violation log depend on. Six surfaces pinned: `is_satisfied_by` preserves the original `required` Vec order in the returned `missing` list across a three-required-walk where the middle one is present in leaf (the operator-facing log and the pic_violations table assume top-to-bottom YAML order — a refactor to HashSet-based diffing for "dedup consistency" would silently scramble the order); `OpsExpression::resolve` short-circuits on the first failing template with that template's UnknownVar name (a refactor to `flat_map` + an aggregated error would silently change the error shape from "Err(first)" to "Err(all)" and break the single-error log contract); `OpsAtom::parse` rejects the empty-middle shape `a::b` AND the empty-scheme shape `:read:file/x` (the existing malformed-shape sweep covered fully empty + no-colon + single-colon + trailing-colon but not the explicit empty-middle — a refactor that switched to `splitn(3, ':')` without the emptiness check would silently produce `OpsAtom { scheme: "a", action: "", object: "b" }`); `OpsAtom` Clone yields independent owned Strings across all three fields via mutation-and-untouched-original (a refactor to `Cow<'static, str>` or `Arc<str>` "for cheaper clone" would surface here at the mutation aliasing back to the original); `OpsExpression` Clone yields an independent `required` Vec via push-on-clone-and-original-untouched (symmetric pin for the Vec wrapper); `OpsParseError` `std::error::Error` trait via dyn-cast + `source() == None` leaf-contract for BOTH variants AND dyn-Display carries the variant-specific operator-grep substring (`Malformed` → "malformed", `UnknownVar(name)` → the name itself). Files:

- [crates/policy-engine/src/ops.rs](../../crates/policy-engine/src/ops.rs) — 6 new tests. `is_satisfied_by_preserves_required_order_in_missing_list` (three-required walk with middle present + first/third missing in that order), `ops_expression_resolve_propagates_unknown_var_from_inner_template` (two-template list, second fails with UnknownVar("path.missing")), `ops_atom_parse_rejects_empty_middle_action_field` (`a::b` + `:read:file/x` both Malformed), `ops_atom_clone_yields_independent_owned_strings` (mutate clone, original untouched across all three fields), `ops_expression_clone_yields_independent_required_vec` (push to clone, original Vec untouched), `ops_parse_error_implements_std_error_trait_for_anyhow_chains` (dyn-cast + source None + Display substring for both variants).

**2026-05-18 (round 98) — 6 more pure-helper tests on proxy crypto/token_cipher.rs from_bytes 31/33-byte boundary + zero-nonce no-panic + CipherError std::error trait + empty-plaintext distinct-nonces + Ciphertext Debug field names + nonce-length AEAD spec pin.** [crates/proxy/src/crypto/token_cipher.rs](../../crates/proxy/src/crypto/token_cipher.rs) already had 12 tests covering the encrypt/decrypt round-trip + distinct-nonces-per-encryption + wrong-key-fails + short-key-rejected + tampered-ciphertext-rejected-by-gcm-tag + wrong-nonce-length-rejected-at-11/13 + Ciphertext Clone independence + BadKeyLen-Display-carries-actual-length + empty-plaintext-round-trip + large-plaintext-round-trip + Aead-Display-stable + empty-key-zero-length — but six operator-actionable surfaces remained unpinned. The TokenCipher is the AES-256-GCM envelope around every persisted Google OAuth token; its key-length boundary contract, nonce-length AEAD-spec contract, and `Ciphertext` Debug grep handle are the load-bearing operator-facing surfaces. Six surfaces pinned: 31-byte and 33-byte `from_bytes` boundary reject with the actual length surfaced (existing pins walk 0/16/empty but not just-off-by-one — operator hex-decode mistakes commonly land on 31 or 33; a refactor that pre-padded to 32 "for robustness" would silently accept truncated keys and the ciphertext would later decrypt against wrong bytes); zero-length nonce rejected via the `ct.nonce.len() != 12` guard without panicking inside `Nonce::from_slice` (a refactor that swapped the order — slicing first, then checking length — would panic on zero-byte slices; pin the no-panic contract via the explicit zero-nonce shape distinct from the existing 11/13-byte pin); `CipherError` `std::error::Error` trait via dyn-cast + `source() == None` leaf-contract for both `BadKeyLen` and `Aead` variants (adapter call sites bubble through anyhow chains at the OAuth-token-persist path — a refactor to a hand-rolled error type without the trait would surface here at dyn-cast rather than far away at `?` call sites); empty-plaintext encryption still yields distinct nonces across two consecutive calls (existing `distinct_nonces` walks `b"hi"`; pin the empty-plaintext symmetric shape since refresh-token-as-empty is a real wire shape — a refactor that special-cased empty plaintexts to a fixed nonce "for speed" would silently break the IV-uniqueness AES-GCM contract); `Ciphertext` Debug renders `Ciphertext`, `nonce`, and `bytes` field names for operator grep (the OAuth persistence failure-triage path traces a Ciphertext shape via `tracing::debug!(?ct, ..)` — a manual Debug that rendered bytes inline as hex without field names would silently strip the operator's grep handle); nonce-is-exactly-12-bytes invariant pinned on the ENCRYPT side across 3 fresh encryptions (existing tests pin the decrypt-rejection side; the AES-GCM spec mandates 96 bits, and a refactor toward a larger nonce "for safety margin" would silently break wire compat with every already-persisted Ciphertext row). Files:

- [crates/proxy/src/crypto/token_cipher.rs](../../crates/proxy/src/crypto/token_cipher.rs) — 6 new tests. `from_bytes_boundary_31_and_33_bytes_rejected_with_actual_length` (both off-by-one shapes, actual length surfaced), `decrypt_with_zero_length_nonce_rejected_without_panic` (length-pre-check contract), `cipher_error_implements_std_error_trait_for_anyhow_chain_walking` (dyn-cast + None-source for both variants), `encrypt_empty_plaintext_still_yields_distinct_nonces_across_calls` (IV-uniqueness symmetric pin), `ciphertext_debug_renders_field_names_for_operator_grep` (Ciphertext / nonce / bytes substrings), `nonce_is_exactly_12_bytes_per_aes_gcm_spec` (AES-GCM 96-bit nonce contract pinned on encrypt side across 3 fresh calls).

**2026-05-18 (round 99) — 6 more pure-helper tests on proxy oauth/state.rs GoogleClient::from_env TOKEN_URL-only override + byte-exact error strings + empty-value vs. unset wire shape + Debug URL-fields + Clone owned-string independence + verbatim URL passthrough.** [crates/proxy/src/oauth/state.rs](../../crates/proxy/src/oauth/state.rs) already had 7 tests covering the `from_env` missing-CLIENT_ID + missing-CLIENT_SECRET error paths, the no-override URL defaults + auth-url-only override + both-override happy paths, the four-field `Clone` value-equality, and the `Debug` `client_id` substring — but six operator-actionable surfaces remained unpinned. The `GoogleClient` is the OAuth router's per-handler state and the boot-path env-var seam; its error-string contract, Clone-independence, and verbatim URL-passthrough are the join points between operator alert filters and the OAuth flow's redirect-URL construction. Six surfaces pinned: `from_env` symmetric token-url-only override path (existing pin walks AUTH-URL-only — a refactor that "consistently" tied the two URLs together would surface here rather than after a regional rollout); `from_env` error strings byte-exact (`"GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID is required"` / `"GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET is required"` — operator boot-failure alert filters key on the exact string, and the existing pins only assert `.contains(env-var-name)` so a pluralization refactor would still pass the substring check while silently breaking every filter); empty-string env-var values accepted as `Ok` (not the `is required` Err path — the wire-shape distinction between unset and empty-string is load-bearing; a `.filter(|s| !s.is_empty())` refactor "to treat empty env var as unset" would silently mask the operator's typo at boot rather than surfacing the actionable downstream error); `Debug` carries `auth_url` AND `token_url` field names AND a representative URL value (symmetric pin to `google_client_debug_carries_client_id` — operators rely on the rendered URLs to confirm regional overrides took effect; a manual Debug that hid the URL fields "for brevity" would silently strip the verification handle); `Clone` yields independent owned Strings across all four fields via mutation-on-original-and-untouched-clone (existing pin only walks value-equality — a refactor to `Arc<str>` "for cheaper clone" would still pass value-equality while changing per-request mutation safety); URL override passthrough preserves trailing-slash AND surrounding whitespace verbatim (`from_env` is a `std::env::var` passthrough — a refactor that started `.trim()`-ing or stripping a trailing slash here "for robustness" would silently change every constructed OAuth URL by one or more bytes). Files:

- [crates/proxy/src/oauth/state.rs](../../crates/proxy/src/oauth/state.rs) — 6 new tests. `from_env_respects_only_token_url_override_with_auth_url_default` (symmetric override path), `from_env_error_strings_are_byte_exact_for_operator_grep` (byte-exact both arms), `from_env_accepts_empty_client_id_and_secret_without_error_path` (empty-vs-unset wire-shape distinction), `google_client_debug_carries_auth_url_and_token_url_field_names` (symmetric Debug pin), `google_client_clone_yields_independent_owned_strings` (mutation independence across all four String fields), `from_env_preserves_url_override_verbatim_including_whitespace_and_trailing_slash` (passthrough contract — no trim, no slash-strip).

**2026-05-18 (round 100) — 6 more pure-helper tests on policy-engine loader.rs PolicyLoadError std::error trait + NotFound Display passthrough + PolicyBundle Debug field names + FilePolicyLoader AsRef<Path> four-shape acceptance + StaticPolicyLoader changed_since post-advance idempotency + Arc-share semantics.** [crates/policy-engine/src/loader.rs](../../crates/policy-engine/src/loader.rs) already had 11 tests covering the FilePolicyLoader yaml-and-version round-trip + mtime-change detection + NotFound mapping + `version_token_sync` sync-vs-async equality + `mtime:<nanos>` format pin, the StaticPolicyLoader `set_yaml` revision-bump + `with_label` override + `rev:0` initial-version + monotonic-bump + yaml-bytes-on-the-wire round-trip + default-impl `changed_since` plumbing, the `PolicyLoadError` per-variant Display + Io-Display passthrough + Backend symmetric, and the `PolicyBundle` Eq differential — but six operator-actionable surfaces remained unpinned. The loader module is the policy-engine's source-of-truth seam; `PolicyLoader` is the pluggable trait the proxy holds as `Arc<dyn PolicyLoader>` for the whole process lifetime, and `PolicyLoadError` is the boundary error the policy_handle reload loop bubbles through `anyhow` chains. Six surfaces pinned: `PolicyLoadError` `std::error::Error` trait via dyn-cast + `source() == None` leaf-contract for all three variants AND Display-via-dyn-cast non-empty (a refactor that swapped `thiserror::Error` for a hand-rolled `impl Display` would silently drop the trait impl, breaking `anyhow::Error::source()` walks at the policy_handle layer — operator-visible as truncated log chains rather than as a compile error); `PolicyLoadError::NotFound` Display passes the path through byte-identically with the `"source not found: "` prefix AND prefix-survives-empty-inner (symmetric to the existing Io passthrough pin — a path truncation refactor "for log brevity" would silently strip the directory half of an operator's mount-point context; an empty-inner gate would produce a bare colon-less log line that operator alert filters key on the colon-space-path shape would miss); `PolicyBundle` Debug carries `yaml` AND `version` field names AND substring values (the policy_handle reload tick traces `?bundle` in a `tracing::debug!` — a manual Debug that hid field names or collapsed `version` into a Display alias would silently strip the operator's `version=` selector that buckets reload events); `FilePolicyLoader::new` AsRef<Path> bound accepts `&str`, `String`, `&Path`, and `PathBuf` AND all four yield byte-identical `path()` + `source_label()` (server.rs builds the loader from an env-var-derived `String`; the embed API may build it from a `&Path` — a tightened `Into<PathBuf>` bound or a `.canonicalize()`-on-construct refactor would surface here as compile break or as label divergence rather than at a downstream caller, and avoids the macOS `/private/tmp/...` canonicalize trap); `StaticPolicyLoader::changed_since` returns `None` when the caller has advanced its tracked version to the current bundle's (idempotent-after-update path — the existing pin walks initial vs. post-edit but doesn't pin the post-advance-no-further-edit boundary; a refactor that conflated "current revision" with "any post-init revision" would silently trigger an engine rebuild storm on every reload-tick after the first edit); `StaticPolicyLoader` Arc-share semantics via wrapping in `Arc<StaticPolicyLoader>` — a `set_yaml` on one handle MUST surface through another handle's `load()` (the proxy holds the loader as `Arc<dyn PolicyLoader>` and clones it into the policy-handle background task; a deep-copy refactor "to isolate test fixtures" would silently make every reload tick read stale YAML while operator edits landed only on the producer-side handle). Files:

- [crates/policy-engine/src/loader.rs](../../crates/policy-engine/src/loader.rs) — 6 new tests. `policy_load_error_implements_std_error_trait_for_anyhow_chain_walking` (dyn-cast + None-source leaf-contract across all three variants), `policy_load_error_not_found_display_passes_path_through_unchanged` (NotFound prefix + byte-exact inner + prefix-survives-empty-inner), `policy_bundle_debug_carries_field_names_for_operator_grep` (yaml + version field names + substring values), `file_loader_new_accepts_str_string_pathbuf_and_path_via_as_ref` (four AsRef<Path> shapes, byte-identical path + source_label), `static_loader_changed_since_returns_none_when_caller_already_tracks_current` (post-advance idempotency across two repeated polls), `static_loader_clone_via_arc_share_set_yaml_visible_across_handles` (Arc-share semantic for set_yaml visibility across handles).

**2026-05-18 (round 101) — 6 more pure-helper tests on proxy crypto/pkce.rs PkceError source-is-None leaf-contract + Debug variant-name pin + byte-vs-codepoint length semantic + empty-challenge no-panic + 43-byte b64url-no-pad invariant + one-byte tail flip Mismatch.** [crates/proxy/src/crypto/pkce.rs](../../crates/proxy/src/crypto/pkce.rs) already had 9 tests covering the RFC 7636 §B example vector + Mismatch + short-verifier-rejected + 42/43-char and 128/129-char boundaries + empty-verifier-length-not-mismatch (fail-closed order) + challenge-case-sensitivity + std::error trait via dyn-cast (Mismatch variant only) + Display-substring stability — but six operator-actionable surfaces remained unpinned. The PKCE verifier is the OAuth callback's load-bearing anti-replay check; its error-chain surface, Debug renders, and length-semantic contract are the join points between OAuth state lookup and operator failure-triage logs. Six surfaces pinned: `PkceError::source() == None` for BOTH variants (existing pin walks only the Mismatch trait surface and only the `to_string()` substring — a refactor that chained an inner cause `Mismatch { inner: sqlx::Error }` "for richer triage" would silently change the anyhow chain walk shape and make the OAuth callback log render two entries instead of one); `Debug` carries variant names for both arms (operators grep `Mismatch` vs. `VerifierLength` to bucket failure modes — a manual Debug that rendered `PkceError(1)` after a numeric-error-code refactor would silently collapse both onto an opaque integer); verifier length counted in BYTES not Unicode codepoints (`str::len()` returns bytes per Rust; a multi-byte UTF-8 string of 43 codepoints = 86 bytes MUST pass length and fall to Mismatch, and 11 codepoints = 22 bytes MUST trip VerifierLength — a refactor to `verifier.chars().count()` "for codepoint correctness" would silently flip both pins); empty-challenge with valid-length verifier yields Mismatch without panicking inside `subtle::ct_eq` (the ct_eq impl returns Choice(0) for length-mismatched slices, but a refactor that pre-asserted `a.len() == b.len()` "for tidiness" would panic on the empty-challenge edge); the b64url-no-pad encoding of SHA-256 is ALWAYS 43 bytes (across three verifier lengths — 43/64/128 — pin both the 43-byte invariant AND the byte-identical-challenge round-trip Ok contract, so a refactor to standard base64 WITH padding (44 chars) or to hex (64 chars) surfaces here before silently overflowing the OAuth state table's `code_challenge` column); one-byte tail flip of a same-length real challenge yields Mismatch (existing `mismatch_rejected` test walks a wholly bogus 5-byte "bogus" challenge that ALSO fails a same-length-check refactor — pin a 43-byte tampered challenge that differs in only the last byte, so a refactor to a length-only short-circuit `if a.len() == b.len() { Ok(()) }` would surface here as a false-Ok). Files:

- [crates/proxy/src/crypto/pkce.rs](../../crates/proxy/src/crypto/pkce.rs) — 6 new tests. `pkce_error_source_is_none_for_both_variants_leaf_contract` (dyn-cast + None-source across Mismatch + VerifierLength), `pkce_error_debug_carries_variant_name_for_operator_grep` (variant-name substring for both arms), `verifier_length_check_counts_bytes_not_unicode_codepoints` (86-byte passes, 22-byte rejects via `é` repetition), `empty_challenge_with_valid_length_verifier_yields_mismatch_not_panic` (length-mismatched ct_eq no-panic contract), `computed_challenge_is_exactly_43_bytes_for_any_input_length` (b64url-no-pad of SHA-256 = 43 chars across 43/64/128 verifier lengths + Ok round-trip), `mismatch_returned_when_challenge_differs_by_one_trailing_byte` (same-length tampered challenge, byte-comparison contract).

**2026-05-18 (round 102) — 6 more pure-helper tests on proxy pic/cache.rs CacheError std::error trait + source-carries-inner-sqlx + CURRENT_PIC_PROFILE structural format + CachedPca Debug field-names + empty-string construction + PcaCache type-level Clone-Send-Sync + multi-predecessor independence.** [crates/proxy/src/pic/cache.rs](../../crates/proxy/src/pic/cache.rs) already had 10 tests covering the CURRENT_PIC_PROFILE byte-exact value + CachedPca::new defaults (including pic_profile) + predecessor-when-present + CacheError Display passthrough + Clone disjoint-buffers across cbor/ops + empty-signature on-construction + i32::MAX hop boundary + Debug pca_id substring + Clone over 2048-element ops vec + #[from] sqlx ?-conversion — but six operator-actionable surfaces remained unpinned. The PCA cache is the bearer middleware's hot-path latency optimization over Trust Plane; CachedPca is the verifier's chain-walk row shape, CacheError is the error-chain boundary the policy_handle bubbles, and CURRENT_PIC_PROFILE is the version-gate every cached row carries (spec.md §15 #11). Six surfaces pinned: `CacheError` `std::error::Error` trait via dyn-cast AND `source() == Some(sqlx_inner)` carrying the inner sqlx::Error's Display verbatim (existing tests pin Display + `?`-conversion but NOT the source-chain walk — a refactor that swapped `#[from] sqlx::Error` for a `String`-stringified inner would still pass Display + `?` while silently collapsing `anyhow::Error::chain()` to a single entry and dropping the actionable sqlx triage from operator logs); CURRENT_PIC_PROFILE structural format (vendor prefix + exactly-one-dot-separator + v1-suffix + non-empty vendor half — the existing byte-exact pin doesn't catch a refactor to `proxilion_v1` underscore or to a UUID; the structural shape is what cross-vendor dashboards bucket on for the eventual `consortium.*` family); `CachedPca` Debug carries `pca_id` AND `ops` AND `hop` AND `predecessor_id` field names AND the hop integer value AND the predecessor UUID (symmetric expansion of the existing pca_id-only pin — operators grep all four selectors to bucket "missing op at hop N" vs. "broken chain link at predecessor M"; a manual Debug that hid the structural names "for brevity" would silently strip every selector but pca_id); `CachedPca::new` accepts empty `cbor` AND empty `p_0` AND empty `ops` without panic AND preserves the empty values through to the struct fields (no `(none)` sentinel substitution — the DB column is NOT NULL but tolerates empty strings, and a refactor that started rejecting empty `p_0` at construction "for hygiene" would surface here as a conscious wire-shape change); `PcaCache: Clone + Send + Sync` at the TYPE LEVEL (the AppState's per-request handler clone path depends on these three bounds — a refactor that swapped `Clone` for `try_clone() -> Result` "for explicit failure" would surface here as a compile break rather than at the AppState layer); ten distinct predecessor UUIDs round-trip independently through ten separate `CachedPca::new` calls AND the test fixture itself produces pairwise-distinct UUIDs (a refactor to a `lazy_static` predecessor sentinel "for speed" would surface here as alias). Files:

- [crates/proxy/src/pic/cache.rs](../../crates/proxy/src/pic/cache.rs) — 6 new tests. `cache_error_implements_std_error_trait_and_source_carries_inner_sqlx` (dyn-cast + source = Some(sqlx) + leaf-source-of-source = None), `current_pic_profile_format_pinned_proxilion_dot_v1_for_grep` (prefix + suffix + exactly-one-dot + non-empty vendor half), `cached_pca_debug_carries_pca_id_ops_hop_and_predecessor_field_names` (four field names + hop value + predecessor UUID substring), `cached_pca_new_accepts_empty_strings_and_vecs_without_panic` (empty-input no-panic + empty-value preservation), `pca_cache_clone_shares_underlying_pg_pool_via_arc` (Clone + Send + Sync type-level bound), `cached_pca_new_round_trips_distinct_predecessor_uuids_independently` (ten-distinct independence + fixture-distinct sanity).
